title: How to get cached images on Bing Images removed that are now broken links? - URLS also
original_question: This is what I have tried so far: I tried putting the Bing image link url in Bing's  online content removal tool.  It didn't work because the in Bing Images URL refers to Bings Image page which is live and working but just a cache of the image.    Then I tried putting the broken link to the image file in the Bing content removal tool - still no luck   I have been trying for 2 weeks now, along with 3 URL's (all broken links) but with no success. Can a member of support please help me, I am desperate to get this fixed.  Thankyou in advance!
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to remove cached images and 3 specific broken URLs from Bing Images, but Bing's content removal tool is not working and support has been completely unresponsive. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Bing Search, Bing Images, Bing Content Removal Tool SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I try to remove the content, the tool fails because the Bing Images URL refers to Bing's Image page, which is technically live and working, even though it is just displaying a cache of the image. The actual source links to the image files are broken. I have been experiencing this issue for over 3 weeks. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Entered the Bing image link URL into the Bing online content removal tool. * Entered the direct broken link to the image file into the Bing content removal tool. * Filled out approximately 10 different support forms. * Sent numerous emails and requests to Bing Support and the DMCA Agent. * Used Google's content removal tool to remove the exact same broken cached links, which worked successfully in about 30 seconds. * Removed all of my Bing accounts, including my Bing Ads account. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have 3 specific broken URLs that need to be removed. The only response I have received during this 3-week period was from a staff member named Charles Sen, which did not resolve the issue (reference thread: https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/bing/forum/bing_websearch-bing_unex_search/how-to-get-cached-images-on-bing-images-removed/a7a42207-705b-4b41-a954-d4f7d34f138e).
accepted_answer: We appreciate your response. Removal requests are usually reflected within 24 hours. If the Bing Content Removal Tool didn't work, we suggest that you post your concern to Bing Listens. It's for the Bing IT specialists and experts to check and prioritized your query as we don't have the proper tools to process your request. Regards.

title: Word docx formatting
original_question: The software is Subscription Product Microsoft Office 365, version 1707 Build  8326.2107 running on a PC using Windows 10. My Word document was created as letter size.  It currently has 55 pages, 192 endnotes, several graphics and headings, and a header. It crashes after search and replace operations.  When it reopens with a recovered file or I open an older version (I save frequently, using modified filenames to avoid overwriting) the page size has changed, the page headers have disappeared and the endnote markers have changed. First is the page size.  It opens as A4 size instead of Letter size.  Bizarre. Second, why do the headers and page numbers disappear?  Inexplicable. Third is the endnote marker.  They were all set to superscript in a small font.  Reopening, they are now Tahoma size 11, without superscript.  If I can't change them all, doing them individually may take as much as three hours (it did Saturday).  Why has the format changed?  Checking the format of a couple of these now wrongly formatted superscript markers, I see they are also marked as +Body.  That is a theme.  If I knew how to change the settings for +Body, I could perhaps simply change that to fix the endnote markers all at once.  How do I change +Body?  It doesn't seem to show up in any Word ribbons and using F1 to open Help and search for a solution, all I get is the usual, irrelevant or outdated information which really isn't very helpful. Word has crashed about ten times since Saturday, usually after a Search/Replace - Select All operation.  This time, when I went to check the version to complete this query, and it crashed when I selected File then Account, it crashed again.  I'm sick and tired of this time-wasting buggy behaviour.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My Word document frequently crashes after search and replace operations, and whenever I reopen the recovered file or an older version, my page size, headers, and endnote formatting are completely altered. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: PC * OS: Windows 10 * Software: Microsoft Office 365 (Subscription Product) * Version: Initially version 1707 Build 8326.2107; recently updated to version 1708 * Add-ins: None * Document Template: "Normal" SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Word has crashed about ten times since Saturday. * Crashes consistently happen after a Search/Replace - Select All operation. Word tells me how many changes were made, and then immediately crashes. * A specific crash trigger is searching to remove a space before all paragraph markers (about 120 instances). This caused three crashes in five minutes. * The application also crashed once when I clicked "File" and then "Account". * When reopening the document after a crash, the page size changes from Letter to A4. * Page headers and page numbers completely disappear. * All 192 endnote markers change from their original small superscript font to Tahoma size 11, without superscript. * The broken endnote markers show up as being marked with the "+Body" theme. * When viewing document errors, only one error shows up identifying the first endnote, but it does not explain what is wrong and is not clickable or expandable. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I save frequently using modified filenames to avoid overwriting, but opening older versions still results in the same formatting changes. * I reset the page size to Letter multiple times before saving, but it continues to open as A4. * I tried manually forcing each endnote marker back to a smaller font and setting them to superscript, but the superscript keeps the marker number below the height of adjacent letters. * I used F1 to search Help for a way to change the "+Body" theme settings to fix all endnotes at once, but found no relevant information. * I updated my Office 365 to version 1708, but the formatting and page size problems persist. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * The document was originally created in Letter size and currently contains 55 pages, 192 endnotes, several graphics, headings, and a header. * Manually fixing the endnote formatting individually takes me up to three hours to complete.
accepted_answer: There is no reason why formatting should change the way you describe it, unless the document is damaged or some poorly designed add-in is interfering with formatting and/or saving in Word.  Ensure that "Automatically update document styles" is cleared in Templates and Add-Ins dialog box.  As far as the paper format is concerned, make sure that you clear the "Scale content for A4 or 8.5 x 112 paper sizes" option.

title: I cant start voice/video calls at my Skype for desktop
original_question: I noticed recently that I can't start a voice/ video calls with any of my contacts using Skype for desktop version at win7 , when I try to call any of them it fail to ring or to initialize the calls  I tried to update to the lastest version which is 7.40.66.103 still not working   I also tried to uninstall and reinstall the app, still not working   P.S: the calls works fine from my android device, I thought the problem was due to that like there would be confusion, so I logout from skype there, still not working  hope you can help me to slove this out.   thanks, have a nice day
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I cannot start voice or video calls with any of my contacts using the Skype desktop application. When I try to call someone, it completely fails to ring or initialize the call. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: PC / Desktop - OS: Windows 7 - Application: Skype for Desktop version 7.40.66.103 - Antivirus: Avira Free 2016 - Browser: Internet Explorer version 11.0.9600.17126 (installed, but not used as my default browser) - Secondary Device: Android phone SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - Voice and video calls fail to ring or initialize. - I cannot search for any new contacts using Skype desktop. - I cannot find the Echo Sound Test (echo123) in my contacts or by searching on my PC. - I can still successfully send messages and files to my existing contacts. - The problem started a few weeks ago. Before that, Skype ran perfectly for years on this exact same OS and hardware. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Updated Skype to the latest version (7.40.66.103). - Uninstalled and reinstalled the Skype desktop app. - Logged out of Skype on my Android device to ensure there was no conflict between devices. - Disabled my Avira Free 2016 antivirus and tried making a call. - Attempted to search for the Echo Sound Test (echo123) on my PC to make a test call, but it would not show up. - Generated a system/diagnostic log and uploaded it here: https://1drv.ms/t/s!AioPfj8n6CxolAL8VlMe0k4PE7Mr ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - The issue started right around the time I installed Skype on my Android phone. - Skype calls and the Echo Sound Test work perfectly fine from my Android device. - Because I can send messages but cannot search or call, I suspect my desktop Skype isn't connected properly to Skype services. - I have intentionally kept my Windows updates disabled for a long time because past updates caused my PC to crash and suffer from slow performance.
accepted_answer: Your Windows 7 installation is missing the last about 3 years Windows updates.   Open Windows Update and install all missing/pending updates incl. updates for Internet Explorer.   Your device drivers are not up-to-date either. Open Lenovo site and make sure that all drivers are updated to the latest available version.   https://pcsupport.lenovo.com/co/en/products/laptops-and-netbooks/lenovo-g-series-laptops/lenovo-g580-notebook/2189/downloads

title: New warning: Shockwave ActiveX Control not compatible
original_question: My Outlook.com is now showing this message, since Patch Tuesday. Did the WUs cause this? I haven't changed any of my settings. Can you help please?  FWIW It's one of three new warnings since Patch Tuesday & I've created a separate thread for each. Is anyone else seeing these messages? MUCatalog Web Control Class (MSComm search) Website File Storage is full (Outlook) Shockwave ActiveX Control not compatible (Outlook)
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am receiving a warning message in Outlook.com stating "Shockwave ActiveX Control not compatible". This issue began immediately after the recent Patch Tuesday Windows Updates. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - OS: Windows 10 - Browser: Internet Explorer - Email Service: Outlook.com - Antivirus: Norton Security (recently uninstalled) - Extensions: Adblock Plus and WinPatrol Plus (neither were installed or enabled when the issue first happened, but both are installed and enabled now) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - I am seeing three new warning messages since Patch Tuesday: 1. "Shockwave ActiveX Control not compatible (Outlook)" 2. "Website File Storage is full (Outlook)" 3. "MUCatalog Web Control Class (MSComm search)" - I am also experiencing an issue where I cannot see any images on websites. Instead, I see the message: "Server Error 404 - File or directory not found. The resource you are looking for might have been removed, had its name changed, or is temporarily unavailable." - Currently, I am unable to recreate the Shockwave warning. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I checked my Internet Explorer ActiveX settings: "Allow Internet Explorer to Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" was already set to Enable. - I verified that "Initialise & Script ActiveX controls marked safe for scripting" is set to Disabled (Recommended). - On September 12th, I uninstalled Norton Security, ran the Norton Removal Tool, and performed a Reset of Internet Explorer. - During the Internet Explorer reset procedure, I checked the box to "Delete personal settings". ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I did not change any of my settings prior to these warnings appearing. - The Windows Updates were installed on September 13th (the day after I did the Norton removal and IE reset). - I have created separate support threads for each of the three new warnings. - The 404 image error has also been reported by another user (Jetta48) in the Performance Problems section, so it may be a wider server issue.
accepted_answer: There is a Flash animation on the page you have open. If you want to see it, you simply have to allow the control to run by clicking the button. This will leave EPM in force.   You can be reasonably sure that ActiveX controls appearing on pages at outlook.live.com are safe to run, but you will want to think twice before allowing controls to run on a site called www.nakedladies.xxx.

title: Excel Sort Limit
original_question: I have a spreadsheet with 100 rows of data.  The sort was not working, so after much trial and error, I discovered that I can sort up to 48 rows.  When I go to 49 it stops sorting.  Because of header rows, I am trying to sort rows 3 to 102.  Rows 3 through 51 will sort.  When I add row 52 it will no longer sort.  My Excel version before 365 appeared to work fine.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have a spreadsheet with 100 rows of data, but I am unable to sort more than 48 rows at a time. When I try to sort 49 rows or more, the sorting function either stops working or causes Excel to crash. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Microsoft Excel (Office 365) Previous Software: An older version of Excel (pre-365), where this issue did not occur Hardware: 8GB of memory installed SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Because of header rows, I am trying to sort rows 3 to 102. Rows 3 through 51 (48 rows) will sort without issue. When I add row 52 (49 rows), it will no longer sort. When I apply a filter and try to sort 49 rows, Excel crashes and restarts. The built-in sort in the filter does not expand to sort all the columns. When I use the sort function in the Data tab at the top of the page, Excel crashes. The total sheet size is 40 columns by 107 rows. The sheet contains one column of VLOOKUPs (100 cells) referencing a range of 2 columns by 17 rows. The sheet also contains three columns of totals/max lookups (300 cells of formulas). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I used trial and error to determine the exact limit is 48 rows. I tried using the built-in sort in the filter, but it wouldn't expand to all columns. I tried using the sort button in the Data tab, which resulted in a crash. I pressed Ctrl+End, which takes me exactly to the bottom right corner of my data and the lookup range, confirming there are no extra unseen columns or rows on the sheet. I made a copy of the spreadsheet and erased all the lookup and formula cells, but I was still limited to sorting only 48 rows. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The sorting functionality worked perfectly fine on my previous version of Excel before I started using Excel 365.
accepted_answer: You are quite welcome, glad I could help. As for the old workbook, I wouldn't bother as long as you have what you need.  Files can become corrupt (well, not corrupt in the traditional sense, but odd rules start to apply to older files that don't make sense or apply to new files), and you never know what is attached to a workbook that you can't see.  The "copy all sheets into new workbook," trick is one I use from time to time just to deal with an excel file that is unusually large or slow compared to how it should be.  I don't know exactly what is happening in that situation, I just know the fix ;) If there was some formula that was broken by doing that trick or you lost some data then it might be worth going back, but...

title: Webpage is not responding
original_question: Many Web Pages are not responding on a regular basis, Windows usually searches for the solution as a Fix It but this is not permanent any suggestions please? Original Title: Persistent not responding
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM Many web pages are persistently failing to respond on a regular basis. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 7 (I previously tried Windows 10 but was not happy with it) Browser: Internet Explorer Search Engine: Bing SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS I receive a "Not Responding" error message. The issue happens completely at random. It is not confined to a particular website; it happens with many different web pages. Windows usually searches for a solution as a "Fix It", but the fix is never permanent. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried an Advanced reset, but it was not successful. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I am connected to the internet via a wireless connection. I use standard internet settings and do not use a proxy. I did not make any changes to my settings prior to this issue starting. I have a multitude of browser add-ons installed and all of them are currently enabled, though none specifically mention IE in their titles or are known by me to be used by IE.
accepted_answer: Hello,  In addition to the Steps listed by Matthew, try the following as well.  Open Internet Explorer in no-add-ons mode and check if you’re able to load the websites. -          Press the ‘Windows key +R’ key on the keyboard. -          In the Run window type ‘iexplore –extoff’ and click ‘OK’. -          Now try to open the websites to check if you’re able to load the websites.  If the issue doesn't persists, then it is sure that add-ons was the reason for this issue.   You can try the steps provided to enable add-ons individually to determine which of the add-ons may be causing the problem.   a) Open Internet Explorer on desktop. b) Click the Tools button, click Manage add-ons, and then click Enable or disable Add-ons c) In the show list, click Add-ons that have been used by Internet Explorer to display all add-ons. d) Click the Add-ons you want to enable, and then select Enable. e) Repeat step (d) for every add-on you want to enable.   For troubleshooting steps on Manage add-ons please refer to this link: http://windows.microsoft.com/en-US/internet-explorer/manage-add-ons#ie=ie-11    Last Step:  You can try to reset Internet Explorer settings to default and check if this helps. -          Press “Windows key + R” to get the run window. -          Type “inetcpl.cpl” and click ok to get the “Internet Options” window. -          Click the “Advanced” tab and click “reset”.

title: Changing guillemets (« angle quotes ») to “sixty-six / ninety-nine” quotes
original_question: Once again, i must appeal to the wisdom and kindness of the clever-user community. I translate lots of PowerPoint presentations from French. These are sometimes full of quotation marks, which in French traditionally look like this: « wibble » I want to make them look like this: “wibble”. Basically, i want to change [guillemet + space] or [guillemet + non-breaking space] to [“] (a sixty-six quote mark). Back in the day, in an earlier version of PPT, i could just use Find&Replace to change, e.g., [« ] to [“]. (In Word, that is still the case). It would take seconds to do it; now it takes minutes. The Find feature in PPT does not distinguish between « " and “. In fact, enter [«] in the search box in the top right of the window and it finds most cases of " and “ but NOT «. Use the Find&Replace box, and it detects a different wrong set of characters. It is a complete mess. I tried a macro that contains the line: oRng.Characters.Replace Chr$(174), "§" (the idea being to subsequently change § to “) Chr$(174) being theoretically [«] – but it doesn't pick up on any instances. (BTW the code works when applied to other characters). Have i just got the wrong character code? How can i find out? Is there no easy way of checking the code for each character in VB? If not, can anyone see what has to be done to get PPT or VBA to recognize [« ] so it can be turned into [“] ? Sorry to be so long - thanks for reading this far! Daddy FrogAmanda
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need to replace French guillemet quotation marks and their adjacent spaces ([« ] or [«] + non-breaking space) with standard English sixty-six/ninety-nine quotes ([“]) in PowerPoint, but the Find & Replace feature no longer recognizes or distinguishes the guillemet characters. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Software: Microsoft PowerPoint (current version is broken; earlier versions worked) * Other Software Mentioned: Microsoft Word (Find & Replace still works correctly here), VBA (for macros), iKey (hot-key software) * Cloud Storage: iCloud (I have an account but have never used it to share files) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When I enter [«] in the search box in the top right of the PowerPoint window, it finds most cases of ["] and [“], but it does NOT find [«]. * When I use the Find & Replace dialog box, it detects a different wrong set of characters. * The Find feature completely fails to distinguish between [«], ["], and [“]. * This replacement process used to take seconds in older versions of PowerPoint, but now it takes minutes. * Find & Replace across multiple documents is also currently broken. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I tried using the standard Find & Replace box to change [« ] to [“], which failed. * I tried using the top-right search box to locate [«], which failed. * I tried writing a VBA macro to replace the character using its code: `oRng.Characters.Replace Chr$(174), "§"` (with the intention of later changing [§] to [“]). I assumed 174 was the code for [«], but the macro didn't pick up any instances. The code structure itself works when applied to other characters. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am a translator working with many French PowerPoint presentations that contain hundreds of these guillemets. * Workaround discovered: I found an inelegant workaround by using `Characters.Replace "«", "§"` directly in VBA (which proves VBA can distinguish [«] from within macros). I then use Find & Replace to remove extra spaces and convert [§ ] to [“], bundling these steps into a single hot-key function using iKey. * I am still looking for a proper, clean way to do this without relying on my dirty mini-routines. * I have a sample file uploaded to iCloud, but I do not know how to share it.
accepted_answer: There's another thing you can try if you're inclined and that is an add-in application for PowerPoint that I have. The add-in is called George.  One of the features it has is to Export formatted text from PowerPoint slides and put it into Word where you can edit/translate the text and then Import the corrected text back into PowerPoint. Again, this all happens while maintaining the formatting of the original text.  Here's a screen clip of the add-ins features.    The feature I've described is in the "Convert..." group.  You can download the add-in for free from my website and it will operate, with restrictions, for free forever. If you find it worth it, you can purchase a license, which removes all operating restrictions.

title: How can I add headings in Headers or Footers?
original_question: Hi, I would like very much to build my headers in a large book with variable contents throughout the book. Is there a way to capture those headings in the Fields shown in "Quick parts"? Or anywhere else so they can be easily retrieved when building my headers? Thanks Serge
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need a way to automatically build page headers for a large book by capturing text that is currently formatted as Headings in the document. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Word 10 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I look in the field references under "Quick Parts", I cannot see any options or fields that refer to my headings. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched through the Fields shown in "Quick Parts" to try and find a way to retrieve and insert my headings into the header area. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My document is a large book about WWII divided into three tomes. The content is organized by years and months (for example, the year 1943, followed by chapters for January, February, March). Because the year and month text are already formatted as Headings in the body of the document, I want the page headers to automatically pull this data and display it dynamically (e.g., "1943 - January", "1943 - February").
accepted_answer: You want the StyleRef Field, not the Ref field.

title: if POWERPNT.EXE is already running on the machine when the application attempts to create a Power Point Application object that the Application contstructor throws an exception
original_question: System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x80080005): Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {91493441-5A91-11CF-8700-00AA0060263B} failed due to the following error: 80080005.

title: Game bar not opening in Windows 10
original_question: I'm running WIn 10 v 1703 (15063.608) on a desktop PC (Win OS on SSD, i7 4790, R9 390, 32 GB DDR3) I have made sure that the slider is set to On in Win 10 Settings > Gaming > Game Bar, and I set a hotkey to Ctrl + Shift + G It still doesn't work. Using Win key +G or my Ctrl + Shift + G doesn't open the game bar. I tried opening games in Fullscreen and the Game bar doesn't open.  I followed instructions to ensure the Dword Value was set to 1 in the follwoing Registry paths: HKEY_CURRENT_USERSOFTWAREMicrosoftWindowsCurrentVersionGameDVRAppCaptureEnabled  HKEY_CURRENT_USERSystemGameConfigStoreGameDVR_Enabled That did nothing also. I then tried downloading and installing "Media Feature Pack for N and KN versions of Windows 10" from Microsoft downloads. That did nothing. Game Bar still doesn't open. How can I get it to open? Thanks
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM The Windows 10 Game Bar does not open when I use shortcuts or launch games. After finding a workaround to make it appear, it still will not stay active while a game is running, and accessing its menu completely locks up my screen. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * OS: Windows 10 v 1703 (build 15063.608) * Device: Desktop PC * Hardware: OS installed on SSD, Intel Core i7 4790, AMD Radeon R9 390, 32 GB DDR3 RAM SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The Game Bar has never worked; this is my first time trying to use it. * Pressing Win + G or my custom hotkey (Ctrl + Shift + G) does nothing. * Opening games in Fullscreen does not trigger the Game Bar. * After reinstalling the Xbox app, the Game Bar flashed on the screen for about a second and then disappeared. * When I finally got the Game Bar to stay open, it disappeared as soon as I launched a game. * I am stuck in a loop: toggling the Game Bar on in Settings minimizes the game. Maximizing the game hides the Game Bar, and hotkeys won't work because the Settings menu is no longer the active window. I cannot have an active game and an active Game Bar at the same time. * Accessing the Game Bar menu settings locks up the screen, requiring me to restart Windows Explorer to get my desktop back (though any settings changes I make do successfully save). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Verified the slider is set to "On" in Win 10 Settings > Gaming > Game Bar. * Set a custom hotkey to Ctrl + Shift + G. * Tried opening games in Fullscreen. * Set the Dword Value to 1 in the following Registry paths: - HKEY_CURRENT_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\GameDVR\AppCaptureEnabled - HKEY_CURRENT_USER\System\GameConfigStore\GameDVR_Enabled * Downloaded and installed the "Media Feature Pack for N and KN versions of Windows 10". * Uninstalled and reinstalled the Xbox app (had to do this twice to get the Game Bar to stay open at all). ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * My hardware easily exceeds the system requirements for Game DVR. * There have been no recent changes to my PC other than standard Windows updates. * Workaround discovered: I can force the Game Bar to appear by toggling the slider from On to Off and back to On in Settings > Gaming > Game Bar. I have to do this reset for every single instance of use. * I have decided to live with this workaround and the Explorer restart glitch for now. I plan to completely reinstall the OS during the next major Windows 10 update rather than spending more time troubleshooting.
accepted_answer: Thank you for your response. For PC games in full-screen mode, you won’t see the Game bar, but you can use the Windows logo key + Alt + R to start and stop recordings. You’ll see the screen flash when the recording starts and completes. You can also use the Windows logo key + Alt + G to record the last 30 seconds if you’ve already turned on background recording.   If the shortcuts do not work for a full-screen game, press Win + G. You’ll see the screen flash twice confirming that the game is recognized. After this, you can then run the game in full-screen again and use the Windows logo key + Alt + R and Windows logo key + Alt + G. If the issue persists, we suggest that you check for new Windows Updates and install the critical and important updates on your computer by following the steps on this link. If your customized shortcuts are not working, you may also check for updates for apps and games from Windows Store by following the steps on this link. Should you need further assistance, you can reply to this post anytime.

title: Why does the size function in Bing Image Search not work any more? In fact, why does Bing Image Search really stink these days?
original_question: When I find an image I like, but it's not the right size, I used to be able to click on the image size in the bottom left hand corner of the screen, and bring up a bunch of other sizes of the exact same image.  I could also bring up other sizes of the same image further down in the search panel.  Now, NONE of it works. The top image size is greyed out rather than being a highlighted link and won't work, and the image selection boxes along the bottom pull up absolutely NOTHING: If I time it quickly, I can refresh the page and click on the top image size link while it is still highlighted and active, but it quickly grays out and goes dead. What is going on here? The Feedback button won't even work. Are you guys working on the site or something? If so, don't you feel ANY obligation to let people know about it? And, please, moderators, do not waste my time and yours by spouting that "clear your cache and restart your browser" nonsense. There is nothing wrong with my computer. I'm using Firefox on Win 7 Pro, and I regularly clear my browser.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am unable to use Bing Image Search to find different sizes of the exact same image. The links and buttons that normally allow me to select multiple size choices for a specific image are either grayed out and unresponsive, or they lead to a page with no results. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 7 Pro Primary Browser: Firefox Secondary Browser tested: Internet Explorer Other devices tested: A friend's computer using Internet Explorer and Google Chrome SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The issue started a couple of weeks ago; before that, everything worked fine. - The underlined image size link in the bottom left corner of the screen is grayed out and dead instead of being a highlighted, active link. - The image selection boxes along the bottom ("Large", "Medium", "Small") and the "Search all sizes" link appear highlighted and active, but clicking them always leads to a dead page with the error message: "We did not find any results for ...". This happens even for pictures that definitely have other size results. - The Feedback button on the Bing site does not work. - I am specifically trying to find different sizes of the *same* image, not use the general menu to filter all search results by size. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I regularly clear my browser cache. - I tested the issue in Internet Explorer on my computer to confirm it isn't just a Firefox problem. - I checked a friend's computer (using IE and Chrome) and got the exact same broken results. - I tried clicking on the overlapping folders, but clicking any of the size icons or the "Search all sizes" link from there still brings me to the dead results page. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I have not made any recent changes to my hardware or software. - I have discovered a temporary workaround: If I refresh the page and click the top image size link as fast as possible (within the first second) while it is still bright and active, I can successfully access the page with multiple sizes before the link grays out and goes dead. However, this requires quick timing and doesn't always work. - I use this feature almost daily to find "Goldilocks" sized (not too big, not too small) funny pictures to post on Fark.com.
accepted_answer: I experience the same results with size greying out once page loads.  1) Hover over the image of choice and click on the overlapping folders.      2) You'll be taken to Main stream screen    3)  Scroll down and you'll see size options when available.    4)  If sizes are not available, you will see this.

title: Office PowerPoint or Word or Excel show EMPTY "File - Open Recent - More"
original_question: After opening Office PowerPoint or Word or Excel (just the application, not any file), they show EMPTY "File - Open Recent - More". No such recent files are displayed.  But if then I open any document, then the applications show such RECENT files in the submenu indicated above.  How to fix it, so that such RECENT files show without requiring to previously open any file.  No problem with other Jessica applications; only with the ones indicated above.  Configuration: Office 2016 version 15.34 on macOS 10.12.5 (16F73) Sierra.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I open standalone Office 2016 applications (Word, PowerPoint, or Excel) directly without opening a specific file, the "File - Open Recent - More" menu is completely empty. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Software: Standalone Office 2016 for Jessica (not Office 365), version 15.34 - OS: macOS 10.12.5 (16F73) Sierra - Devices: MacJessica mini and iMacJessica - Storage: Local booting disks only (Internal Apple Fusion Drive on the Jessica mini, external Samsung Portable SSD T3 on the iMacJessica). I do not use cloud computing. - Account Setup: Admin account with automatic login. I do not use a password to log into MacJessica or for any Office applications. - Browser (used for forum): Safari 10.1.1 (12603.2.4) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The "File - Open Recent - More" menu shows no recent files when launching the application directly. - Right-clicking the application icon on the Dock also shows an empty "Open Recent" list. - The issue happens consistently across Word, PowerPoint, and Excel. - There is no problem with other "Jessica" applications; it only affects Office. - I am unsure exactly when it started, but I suspect it began when I started using Office 2016. - Forum upload bugs: When trying to share screenshots of the empty menus, drag-and-drop failed (files disappeared after posting), the "Insert Image" icon gave an "Invalid file type. Please select another" error, and dragging/dropping JPEGs took over the whole screen and deleted my typed text. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I previously posted this exact issue on the Microsoft forums (thread 9cdb0206-1bc0-4a69-a601-4ab8eb02226d) but nobody answered. - I posted the current issue in the individual Word, PowerPoint, and Excel forums since there is no general Office forum for multiple applications. - I uploaded my screenshots (PDFs and JPEGs) to an external image host (postimg.org) and linked them in the forum using double carriage returns to format them properly. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - Workaround 1: If I double-click an existing document to open the application, the "Open Recent" menu populates correctly. - Workaround 2: If I open the application by opening a file first, and then close that document, the recent files still show up. - Workaround 3: If I open the application and use "File - Save As" to save the default empty document to my Desktop as "Document.docx", the "File - Open Recent" menu immediately shows my recent files. - All workarounds are temporary; the empty menu problem returns as soon as I quit and reopen the application.
accepted_answer: Hi JessicaTop,   Please follow the steps below to see the result: If the issue persists, please provide the Office version for our more investigation. Please go to Word>About Word to check the version.   Thanks, RenaWendy

title: Struggling with a MERGEFIELD
original_question: Hello there, I'm currently in the process of creating a MERGEFIELD and have the following script down ( I have used the yes no fields as an example) { { IF{ MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes1 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes2 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes3 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes4 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes5 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes6 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes7 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes8 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes9 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes9 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD yes } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes10 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno1 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno2 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno3 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno4 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno5 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno6 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno7 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno8 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno9 } } { IF{MERGEFIELD no } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno10 } } When I merge the document, if the field = 1, then instead of placing the merged field at the top of the screen, it will place it with 10 blank spaces and then start with the information, what can I do so that when I do merge the document, no matter which field, ie 0 or 1, it will place the information at the top of the screen? I hope this makes sense! Thanks
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am creating a mail merge document with conditional IF statements to display either an office address or a home address, but when the second condition is met, the document inserts 10 blank lines at the top of the page, pushing the text near the center instead of keeping it in the top left corner. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Word SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - I have two separate addresses (Office and Home) with 10 lines of code each (e.g., Office1 to Office10, and Home1 to Home10). - The address format requires separate lines for Address 1, Address 2, City, County, and Postcode. - My script checks a merge field called `iscorrect`. If it equals "0", it merges the `Doubleyes` (Office) fields. If it equals "1", it merges the `Doubleno` (Home) fields. - When `iscorrect` = "1", the document leaves 10 blank spaces at the top of the screen where the "0" condition fields were skipped, and then starts printing the Home address information. - I need the resulting address to always appear in the top left-hand corner without any blank spaces caused by the skipped mail merge fields. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I wrote a script using individual IF statements for every single line. My code looks like this: `{ { IF{ MERGEFIELD iscorrect } = "0" { MERGEFIELD Doubleyes1 } } ... { IF{MERGEFIELD iscorrect } = "1" { MERGEFIELD Doubleno1 } } }` (repeated for all 10 lines of both addresses). - I tried an alternative block of code, but when I press Alt+F9, the code completely disappears and does not show the values of the mail merge. My individual IF statements successfully pull the data, but they create the blank space issue. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - The final output must have the address information on separate lines for my client.
accepted_answer: There were a couple of typos in my previous reply - now corrected I hope.  I've posted a couple of sample Word documents at https://goo.gl/YRNUkC to show how to do this.  Once you have downloaded and unzipped that (if necessary), you will need to reconnect each test .docx to the mmds1.docx test data source.  test1.docx shows the simpler thing I was originally describing. test2.docx shows the type of thing you need to attack the address problem. However, I have stuck to using the "doubleyes" and "doubleno" field names rather than office1 etc.  If you're not seeing anything with Alt-F9 it's sometimes because the fields haven't been updated and sometimes because there's an extra pair of { } surrounding everything.  The syntax for the home/office problem is more like this (using the doubleyes/doubleno field names):  { IF [ MERGEFIELD iscorrect } = "0" "{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleyes1 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleyes1 } " }{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleyes2 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleyes2 } " }{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleyes3 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleyes3 } " }etc.{ IF [ MERGEFIELD iscorrect } = "1" "{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleno1 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleno1 } " }{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleno2 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleno2 } " }{ IF { MERGEFIELD doubleno3 } <> "" "{ MERGEFIELD doubleno3 } " }etc.

title: One tab, but different "print zoom" level per manual page break?
original_question: Hello, Please forgive that I have asked this in another forum already, but I have received no responses there, and it has been out there for several weeks now. My hope is that maybe someone in this forum possibly has some ideas? I have been trying a few different things, and have done some research, but haven't yet found an answer to this question.  I have a total of 71 Workbooks with 2 tabs each, and they all need to be set up for printing. While I have set everything up for printing as best I could, some of them print out really small.  I'll provide an example. I've set the page breaks manually for all of these. One sheet/tab has the following:  A1:I30 that needs to print as page 1. A31:F64 needs to print as page 2. A65:F88 needs to print as page 3. A89:H135 needs to print as page 4. A136:G167 needs to print as page 5. A168:J202 needs to print as page 6. A203:I239 needs to print as page 7. A240:G264 needs to print as page 8.  Now, that's only one tab, and literally every tab has different ranges for each page.  The challenge is that when everything is adjusted to fit the largest area, the percentage shrinks for everything.  Is there a way to make each manually created page on this one tab (as described above) print as a different percentage for each manually created page? (So that each range above prints at a different percentage?) So, for example (and I'm using random percentages here for illustration purposes), if say the defined range that makes page 8 could print as 100% and fit on one page, but page 6 maybe needs to be 40% to fit on one page, and say page 2 could fit at 85% for one page, is this possible to accomplish?  Note: Everything needs to remain on the one tab.  So, right now with those above ranges for this example, all pages print at 40% when not all pages otherwise would not need to print out that small; the only reason all pages print out at 40% now is because of accomodating for the largest area (manually created page). (Note: I have also tried setting print areas manually as well and that didn't seem to make a difference.)  Thanks in advance for any assistance you can provide.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have 71 Excel workbooks (with 2 tabs each) where I need different manual page breaks on the same tab to print at different zoom percentages. Currently, Excel applies the smallest zoom percentage needed for the largest print area to the entire tab, causing smaller ranges to shrink unnecessarily. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Excel File formats used: .xlsx, .xlsm SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - When adjusting the print settings to fit the largest area on a tab, all pages on that tab shrink to that same percentage (e.g., 40%). - Smaller areas that could easily fit on a page at 85% or 100% print out extremely small and are hard to read, leaving excessive white space. - When attempting to use macros, I receive the following security warning: "Privacy warning: This document contains macros, ActiveX controls, XML expansion pack information, or Web components. These may include personal information that cannot be removed by the Document Inspector." - When testing macro codes, an array formula displayed a red line error due to a double parenthesis `((`. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Manually set page breaks for all sheets. - Manually set print areas (this made no difference). - Tested four different macro codes and saved the file as an .xlsm. - Printed the .xlsm version and the original .xlsx version side-by-side to compare; there was no visible difference. - Tested the 4 macro codes under three different conditions: with original page breaks in place, after resetting page breaks and setting print areas, and after resetting page breaks completely to see if the code would "take over". - Corrected a syntax error in one of the macro codes by removing an extra parenthesis (changing `((` to `(`), which cleared the red error line. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - Everything must remain on a single tab; I cannot split the data into multiple tabs. - Every tab across the 71 workbooks has different ranges for each page. - An example of the varying ranges on a single tab: Page 1 (A1:I30), Page 2 (A31:F64), Page 3 (A65:F88), Page 4 (A89:H135), Page 5 (A136:G167), Page 6 (A168:J202), Page 7 (A203:I239), Page 8 (A240:G264). - These documents will be widely distributed to users with limited Excel knowledge, so macro security warnings popping up are not an acceptable outcome. - Because a working print solution hasn't been found, we are currently planning to strongly encourage users to view the files electronically instead of printing them.
accepted_answer: The code sets the print area.  In the command  Set r1 = ws.Range("A1:I30,A31:F64,A65:F88,A89:H135,A136:G167,A168:J202,A203:I239,A240:G264") in the code I posted and in the command  vRng = Array(("A1:I30", "A31:F64", "A65:F88", "A89:H:135", "A136:G167", "A168:J202", "A203:I239", "A240:G264") on the code Tasosk posted. (and there is an extra left paren which you have correctly removed).  These will be the pages if the Zoom settings are set to fit that area on a single page.  Then each of the codes has the command  ...PrintArea = where it passes the address of the range for the specific area being printed.   You should remove all manual pagebreaks because scaling does not override pagebreaks like Fit to page does.  To summarize -   no manual page breaks   no printareas defined.    Put percent scaling into the arrays to correspond to the range areas - determined by doing it manually first (but if you can't see the page data doing it manually, then using the same scaling with the code will be no better.   -- Regards, Trevor Ogilvy

And do not accept the "Pay for version" Trial option during installation  Download, install, update and scan your System with the free version of Malwarebytes AntiMalware:: http://www.malwarebytes.org/products/malwarebytes_free  @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  You can clean up your Hard Drive using these two methods.  "Delete files using Disk Cleanup" http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/delete-files-using-disk-cleanup#delete-files-using-disk-cleanup=windows-8  Using the Free CCleaner Program: http://www.piriform.com/ccleaner  Under NO condition use the Registry cleaning Option in CCleaner  @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  Make sure that you have the latest updated Drivers:  Go to your Computer or Hardware Manufacture's website > Driver and Software Support Section > look up your Computer or Hardware Model number > then your Operating System > locate the correct Drivers > download and install them.  @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  "How to Optimize the Performance of Windows 8 and 8.1"  http://www.eightforums.com/tutorials/37511-optimize-performance-windows-8-a.html   @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  This is worthwhile reading too:  "How to Optimize Windows 8.1 for a desktop PC" http://www.howtogeek.com/167013/how-to-optimize-windows-8.1-for-a-desktop-pc/  @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  "Performance & maintenance" http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/performance-maintenance-help#performance-maintenance-help=windows-8&v1h=win8tab1&v2h=win7tab1&v3h=winvistatab1  @@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@  And I would check to see if there are corrupted System Files. Each Method/step below progresses to the next if the first does not fix any corrupted files that are found, etc.  "Use the System File Checker tool to repair missing or corrupted system files" https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/kb/929833  _________________________________________________  And these eightforums.com Tutorials are very detailed for problems resulting from SFC Scan:  "How to Run "SFC /SCANNOW" Command at Boot or in Windows 8 and 8.1" http://www.eightforums.com/tutorials/3047-sfc-scannow-command-run-windows-8-a.html  Read this Section there in conjunction with the rest of the information they give in above link.    Note If SFC could not fix something, then run the command again to see if it may be able to the next time. Sometimes it may take running the sfc /scannow command 3 times restarting the PC after each time to completely fix everything that it's able to.  If not, then run the Dism /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth command to repair any component store corruption, restart the PC afterwards, and try the sfc /scannow command again.  If still not, then try using OPTION ONE above to run the sfc /scannow command at boot  If still not, then you could do a refresh of Windows 8, or run a System Restore using a restore point dated before the bad file occured to fix it. You may need to repeat doing a System Restore until you find a older restore point that may work.  If still not, then you can use the steps in the TIP box in the OPTION THREE section to manually replace the files that SFC could not fix. __________________________________________________ "How to Refresh Windows 8 and Windows 8.1" http://www.eightforums.com/tutorials/2293-refresh-windows-8-a.html Refreshing your PC goes like this: The PC boots into Windows RE (Recovery Environment). Windows RE scans the hard drive for your data, settings, and apps, and puts them aside (on the same drive). Windows RE installs a fresh copy of Windows. Windows RE restores the data, settings, and apps it has set aside into the newly installed copy of Windows. The PC restarts into the newly installed copy of Windows. _________________________________________________ "How to Reset Windows 8 and Windows 8.1" http://www.eightforums.com/tutorials/2302-reset-windows-8-a.html "When you reset your PC, the drive will be completely reset, and all your apps and files will be removed from it. Windows 8 will be reinstalled, and you'll need to change any settings and reinstall any apps that you want to get back after resetting. If you know if any files that you don't want to lose, then you should back those files up before starting the reset" Cheers.

title: Bing has changed the title of our charity's website
original_question: Our charity's website (https://JenniferAnn.org) is not showing correctly in Bing search listings. The site is properly configured with the title of "Jennifer Ann's Group" which is the name of our charity founded in 2006. However Bing has changed the listing to instead show "Murder of Jennifer Ann Crecente - Official Site." Unfortunately, yes, Jennifer Ann Crecente was murdered in 2006 and it was her death which prompted the formation of this 501(c)(3) organization but this does not excuse Bing unilaterally misrepresenting us in their search results. Our work is not focused on Jennifer's murder but rather helping other young people recognize and avoid abusive dating relationships. When people search for information about "Jennifer Ann's Group" they should be able to find our website - not be presented with an "official site" for Jennifer's murder. For two days we have sent multiple requests to Bing / Microsoft through their automated systems as well as to their Twitter accounts in an attempt to correct the manner in which our organization is being misrepresented. There has been no response. Is there an official process to correct this? Or a person who can assist? Is there a procedure we can follow to ensure this does not happen again? While we recognize that this may not seem like a significant issue to some, it is terribly frustrating and hurtful for those of us who miss Jennifer and are working every day to help other adolescents avoid her fate. The fact that Bing would ignore our content and replace our name with one of its own choosing is genuinely distressing and offensive. The work we do is very important to us and it is not acceptable for Bing to define our work like this. Please note: we are aware of the information regarding Bing's selection of a title for a web page (https://blogs.bing.com/webmaster/2014/06/23/how-does-bing-choose-the-title-for-my-web-page). Our HTML and associated metatags / title / content reflect our name as "Jennifer Ann's Group." Bing appears to have used content from Wikipedia to select the title they have applied.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM Bing search results are displaying an incorrect and offensive title for my charity's website, completely ignoring my site's configured title. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Platform: Bing Search Website URL: https://JenniferAnn.org SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS My website's HTML, associated metatags, title, and content are all properly configured to show the name "Jennifer Ann's Group". Instead of my configured title, the Bing search listing displays: "Murder of Jennifer Ann Crecente - Official Site." Bing appears to be ignoring my site's code and using content from Wikipedia to unilaterally generate this incorrect title. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I verified my site's configuration against Bing's official documentation regarding how Bing chooses web page titles. I sent multiple requests to Bing/Microsoft through their automated systems over the course of two days. I reached out to Bing and Microsoft Twitter accounts to request a correction. I called the Bing Support number, which routed me to Bing Ads. I spoke with a Bing Ads manager who confirmed my support ticket number is in their system and that two different groups are aware of the problem. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My organization is a 501(c)(3) charity founded in 2006. While the charity was formed following the 2006 murder of Jennifer Ann Crecente, our work focuses on helping young people avoid abusive dating relationships. The auto-generated title misrepresents our focus and is highly distressing. It has been five days since I first reported the problem, and I have received zero direct response from Bing/Microsoft. The first person I spoke with at Bing Ads stated that the only way to get this changed might be to send a "cease and desist" letter to Microsoft's legal department. The Bing Ads manager I spoke with estimated it would take about 5 days for the problem to be addressed.
accepted_answer: Hi Daniel,  This issue may occur from search engine conflicts.  For better assistance, we recommend contacting Bing Support. Check this link for more information on how to contact Bing Support.  Feel free to post back if you have other concerns.

title: Can we Sort Bing Search Results by date?
original_question: Is there any way to sort search results by date.  I checked the MSD page for bing: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff795620.aspx But nothing is mentioned there.  Could there be an undocumented search parameter I could use?  In this situation, bing's estimation of "relevance", I want to only look at results for this month and last, because that is when the issue was being posted. This question applies to search results both for native Bing searches and for the bing searches built into the forum ie these 2011 results are totally irrelevant to my query
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to find a way to sort Bing search results strictly by date, but I cannot find an option or search parameter to do this. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Product: Bing Search (applies to both native Bing searches and Bing searches built into forums) * Browser: Mozilla Firefox SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Bing's default "relevance" sorting shows totally irrelevant results from as far back as 2011. * When using Bing's predefined date limits, the results are still not sorted by date within that selected range. * When I try to use the Bing feedback link in Firefox, the text box is broken and will not let me type anything into it. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I checked the MSDN page for Bing (https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/ff795620.aspx) but found no mention of date sorting. * I searched for an undocumented search parameter I could use. * I tried using the predefined date limits, but they are very limited and do not sort the results within the range. * I checked the Bing help page but did not find anything useful. * I tried to submit feedback via the feedback link, but encountered the typing bug in Firefox. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I specifically want to restrict my search to results from "this month and last month" because that is when the issue I am researching was posted. * I am familiar with Microsoft's UserVoice "suggestion box" sites and have compiled a large list of feature suggestions for other MS products (including Word, Windows 10, Office 2016, Visio, OneNote, Access, and Excel) which I shared in the forum.
accepted_answer: Hi, David007,  It's been requested.  You can add your vote to Bing Listens.  Does this help?  Query:  site:answers.microsoft.com RohnDavid007 office oem license  These are the results I received by adding (Dec/2015 -Jan/2016) to your terms above.

original_question: In Office 2016 - Word 15.38 for Mac -  Comboboxes fail because the Userform is in front of the ComboBox items. See screenshots from 2011 vs 2016. This is also a problem for Excel 15.38 .

title: Copying data into PowerPoint, losing slide number
original_question: I'm using PowerPoint 365 on Win10. I was creating a presentation where I had previously numbered the slides. I wanted to re-use slides not only from another preso I had but also input from someone else. In each of the cases where I copied information in, the slide numbers would not render no matter what I did. My only solution was to create a new slide after the "bad" one, copy data in and then I had my slide number. So the only way I can be sure to get slide numbers to render - if copying from another deck - is to copy it in once, then copy it again. This seems crazy. What, if anything, am I doing wrong here? This seems like it'd be pretty straightforward.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I copy slides from other presentations into my current presentation, the slide numbers fail to render on the newly pasted slides. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Software: PowerPoint 365 (I have installed the latest 365 updates) - Operating System: Windows 10 - Device: Lenovo laptop (purchased within the last few months) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - Slide numbers do not appear on slides copied from other decks, whether they are from my own previous presentations or files provided by someone else. - There are absolutely no error messages when this happens, making the issue difficult to notice. - I am not sure exactly when the problem started; it was pointed out to me by someone reviewing my presentation. - The problem is not isolated to a single presentation file. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I installed the latest Office 365 updates. - I ran an experiment where I created a new blank presentation, added a couple of slides, inserted slide numbers (apply all), and then copied in slides from two different presentations. The slide numbers did not appear on the copied slides. - I tried telling the system to insert slide numbers again after the slides were copied, but it had no impact. - I ran the experiment again, but this time I inserted the slide numbers AFTER I copied the slides over. This was slightly better, as one of the two copied slides successfully added a slide number. - I tried variations of copying in a very simple slide (slide 4) that had nothing in the bottom part of the slide that might interfere with a header, but this did not work. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - My only working workaround is to create a brand new slide immediately after the "bad" copied slide, and then copy the data into that new slide. The slide number will then render properly. - To guarantee that slide numbers appear when bringing in content from another deck, I essentially have to copy the slide in once, then copy its contents again into a new slide, or completely recreate the slides from scratch.
accepted_answer: Thank you for the detailed response.  We tried to replicate your issue on our end, according to the description you provided and we had the same results. The copied slides from the other PowerPoint presentation file doesn't have slide number. However, we were able to bring back the slide number by following the steps below:  Let us know if you need further assistance.

title: Linking a graph from Excel into Word
original_question: My version of Office is MS Office 365 ProPlus, v. 16.0.7466 I have data in Excel, which I've graphed - in this example, a pie chart. If I copy and paste the from Excel using Ctrl+C > Ctrl+V, I end up with a graph that looks like I want it to. In fact, I can not only easily alter the size of the contents within it. (for example, if I want to adjust the legend so it's a bit smaller, I can. But, what I really want is to link it. So, in Word, I use Paste Special > Paste link, and choose "Microsoft Excel Chart Object." Then click ok. It pastes - it's linked. I can double-click on it and bring up the chart in Excel. This is good. But I lose the ability to fine tune it - such as alter the size of the legend within it. If I do it within Excel, it doesn't seem to update the image within Word. My next step was to Paste with Link, but using the "MSO Graphic Object." At that point, I seem to lose the link even though I specified to link. (Trying an Alt+F9 seems to bear this out - no link is displayed.) Ideally, I'd like to have the true link, as I use this report as a template, so I can do a search and replace and replace text within the to point to another file. (It works great when I do it!) Am I doing something incorrectly, is there a bug, or is this a setting I haven't found? Is there a good primer I can read on OLE/Cutting/pasting that would clear things up? Thank you. Rich
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to link an Excel chart into a Word document so I can use the document as a template and update the source file paths via Search and Replace. However, depending on the paste method I use, I either lose the ability to fine-tune the chart's formatting in Word, or I lose the OLE link and path visibility entirely. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Primary Device: PC on a government network * Primary Software: MS Office 365 ProPlus, v. 16.0.7466 * Secondary Device: Personal laptop * Secondary Software: MS Office 2010 (Professional Plus), v. 14.0.7188 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When pasting via Ctrl+C > Ctrl+V: The graph looks correct and I can alter the size of contents (like the legend) directly in Word. However, right-clicking the object does not show an "Update" command, and pressing Alt+F9 does not display the full file path. (It will only update if I choose the "Edit Data" command from the right-click menu). * When pasting via Paste Special > Paste link > "Microsoft Excel Chart Object": The chart links successfully (double-clicking brings it up in Excel), the right-click menu shows the "Update" command, and Alt+F9 reveals the file path. However, I lose the ability to fine-tune the chart elements (like the legend size) within Word. Furthermore, if I adjust the formatting within Excel, it doesn't seem to update the image within Word. * When pasting via Paste Special > Paste link > "MSO Graphic Object": The link is lost completely, even though I specified to link it. Pressing Alt+F9 confirms no link path is displayed. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Created a pie chart in Excel and copied it using Ctrl+C. * Pasted the chart into Word using standard Ctrl+V. * Pasted the chart into Word using Paste Special > Paste link > "Microsoft Excel Chart Object". * Pasted the chart into Word using Paste Special > Paste link > "MSO Graphic Object". * Used Alt+F9 to check for visible link paths across the different paste methods. * Attempted to resize the chart legend directly in Word after pasting. * Attempted to format the chart in Excel to see if the visual changes would push to the linked Word document. * Replicated the exact same test and results on my personal laptop running Office 2010 to see if it was a version-specific bug. * Captured screenshots of the Paste Special dialog box showing the different paste methods. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * My primary PC is on a government network, which may restrict my ability to change certain system settings. * My ultimate goal is to have a "true link" where I can see the file path using Alt+F9. This is because I use this Word report as a template; I need to be able to do a Search and Replace on the path text to point to another Excel file, and then simply update the links so all graphics refresh automatically.
accepted_answer: Hi Rich,   By linking "Microsoft Excel Chart Object" you are good to bind data from Excel chart to Word chart. Physical attributes are localized like Length/Breadth etc. within Excel/Word.   Linking will bind data only between Excel and Word individually not the other local attributes like length/breadth of chart object. So, if you change the size of the legend in Excel it will not reflect in Word.   Regards, Daniel

title: ctrl-p doesn't invoke print menu
original_question: Anyway to get this back?  I've websearched this, but only postings I've found have to do with hotkeys in the File menu or in the Quick Access menu.  I don't have Print in the Quick Access menu, nor do I want it, and the File menu is not a menu (it is an entire panel without any indication of hot keys of any sort).

The issue is intermittent and spurious; it is not always present but keeps resurfacing. The File menu displays as an entire panel without any indication of hotkeys of any sort. accepted_answer: It could be caused by interference from an add-in. That's the usual explanation.  This can be unexpected and puzzling. I have Ctrl+Shift+X assigned to the multiplication symbol (×) in both Word and Outlook, but this morning I was writing an email, and every time I tried to insert the ×, I got a message about the "fax service." I ended up having to insert the × in Word and copy/paste it because (as usual) it was being elusive in the Symbol dialog. I cannot now reproduce the problem; if I press Ctrl+Shift+X in either a Plain Text or Rich Text email, it just inserts the × without demur. I have no idea what was causing the issue.

title: Embedded video stops playing
original_question: Hello everyone, We have Office 365 installed on a 55" touch screen monitor with an embedded computer. The computer is running Windows 7. We are running a power point file that has an embedded video. The power point is set up to be an interactive presentation to be used at trade shows. This week I had to change the the account for the Office 365 from a business version to a home version. So I ended up deleting the old version of Office 365 and then downloading & installing the home account version. Afterwards I discovered that the one embedded video that is in the presentation would stop at random places during playback. What you would see is the cursor show up in the middle of the video & the video would stop. Clicking on the video would start it right back up. We have tried forcing the cursor off the video, unplugging the mouse & the keyboard, nothing helps. This video had ran without problems before now. Any thoughts

accepted_answer: For us to further isolate the issue, we suggest that you create a test user profile and check if the same problem will occur. In such cases creating a new one will help us to identify if the issue is specific to a corrupted user profile. You can refer to this link for the steps.   Let us know how it went for you.

title: Saving a Power Point 2016 Custom Show in pptx format
original_question: Hello, If I have a pptx file that has 2 custom shows: Show 1: slides 1 and 3 Show 2: slides 2 and 3 How can I save Show 1 into a new pptx file that has slides 1 and 3 from the original file? Many thanks. Keith
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need to find a way to save a specific Custom Show from a PowerPoint presentation into a new .pptx file that contains only the slides included in that Custom Show. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: PowerPoint 2016 File format: .pptx WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I am planning to write custom code to handle this extraction, though I won't have time to work on the code for the next several days. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My actual presentation is around 70 slides long and contains 4 Custom Shows. As an example of my goal: if the original file has Show 1 (slides 1 and 3) and Show 2 (slides 2 and 3), I want to save Show 1 into a new file that only has slides 1 and 3. My files do not contain the two specific edge-case situations discussed in the thread, so my custom code will not need to account for them.
accepted_answer: Have a go with this.  It'll save the slides from each of your custom shows out to a separate presentation.  Watch out for linebreaks that the forum editor "helpfully" introduces. Option Explicit  Sub WhoIsInSlideShow()          Dim oPres As Presentation     Dim oCopyPres As Presentation     Dim lShowNum As Long     Dim x As Long          Set oPres = ActivePresentation          With oPres         ' Don't do anything if there are no custom shows!         If .SlideShowSettings.NamedSlideShows.Count > 0 Then                      ' We have shows; work with each of them in turn:             For lShowNum = 1 To .SlideShowSettings.NamedSlideShows.Count                                  oPres.SaveCopyAs oPres.Path & "\" & "SHOW_" & CStr(lShowNum) & "_" & oPres.Name                 Set oCopyPres = Presentations.Open(oPres.Path & "\" & "SHOW_" & CStr(lShowNum) & "_" & oPres.Name)                                           With oCopyPres                     For x = .Slides.Count To 1 Step -1                         ' is this slide part of the current custom show?                         ' if not, delete it                         If Not IsInCustomShow(.Slides(x).SlideID, lShowNum) Then                             .Slides(x).Delete                         End If                     Next                     .Save                     '.Close                 End With    ' copy presentation                              Next    ' custom show         End If  ' has custom shows     End With   ' oPres, current presentation  End Sub  Function IsInCustomShow(lSlideID As Long, lShowNum As Long) As Boolean     Dim x As Long     For x = 1 To UBound(ActivePresentation.SlideShowSettings.NamedSlideShows(lShowNum).SlideIDs)         If lSlideID = ActivePresentation.SlideShowSettings.NamedSlideShows(lShowNum).SlideIDs(x) Then             IsInCustomShow = True             Exit Function         End If     Next End Function

title: Laptop Freezes
original_question: Hi there, I am running Windows 8.1 on my laptop, every Wednesday at 12.33pm my screen freezes and becomes unresponsive, I can usually fix it by restarting, that can take up to 5 minutes,  but sometimes I have to do a System Restore, is there something I can do to stop this problem, hope you can help. Thanks, Rick.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My laptop screen freezes and becomes completely unresponsive at the exact same time every week. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 Device: Laptop SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The screen freezes and becomes unresponsive exactly every Wednesday at 12:33 pm. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I usually restart the laptop to fix the freeze, which can take up to 5 minutes. When a restart is not enough, I have to perform a System Restore. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT Restarting and running a System Restore are my only current workarounds to regain control of the laptop, but the issue always returns the following Wednesday.
accepted_answer: Hi Rick - If it's for sure scheduled then that could only be Task Scheduler. Type that in Start Search, click on the Library in left pane, scroll down in center pane to find the task responsible, right click to Disable it. Report back the exact task or google it to find what it does and if it's needed. But many who have disabled all of them find they experience nothing other than better performance. I would want to know what they are first, though. Feel free to ask back any questions and let us know how it goes. I'll keep working with you until it's resolved.

title: export to CD option in ppt2016
original_question: The embedded music doesn't play on powerpoint 2016 when I exported it to a memory stick-  I used mp3's '
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I created a PowerPoint presentation with embedded MP3 background music and exported it to a memory stick using the "Export to CD" option, but the music will not play when I open the presentation on a different computer. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Original Computer Software: PowerPoint 2016 Second Computer Software: PowerPoint 2013 Media Format: MP3 audio files Hardware: Memory stick (USB drive) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The presentation and music play perfectly on the original computer where I created it. When I move the memory stick to the second computer, there is no music. There are no pop-up error messages. The music icon still appears on the slides, but when I hover over the icon, it says "cant link to music". WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I used the "Export to CD" option and selected the memory stick as the destination. I manually placed the MP3 files into the same folder as the presentation and reloaded them. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The presentation was made for a funeral and contains 200 pictures with 5 MP3 files intended to play in the background.
accepted_answer: Try this:  Delete the existing music files Put the music files in the same folder as the PowerPoint file Re-insert them  That generally produces "pathless" links that work on other systems so long as:  - The PPT/x and music files stay together in the same folder and - The computer is capable of playing the music files in the first place  But something else to check on ... see, it's odd that PPT should be complaining about linked files. The default nowadays is for it to embed music and video files, not link them, unless you explicitly told it to link (in which case, re-insert them, DON'T link and you should be ok).  Unless perhaps you're playing the presentation on an older version of PowerPoint; it might not be able to play embedded MP3s.  Something else to check ...

original_question: Recently I am logged out my skype account and a message was displaying that i need to update the application. But after updating skype doesn't start. I have updated the directx and .net framework but nothing is helping me to start the program. Please suggest me a solution.

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: Official (work) laptop - Operating System: Windows 7 64-bit - Application: Skype for Windows Desktop - Browser: Internet Explorer SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The issue started when I was unexpectedly logged out of my Skype account and a message displayed saying I needed to update the application. - After updating, the program does not open at all. - The issue presents as a "Skype.exe Application Error". accepted_answer: Windows installation on your computer is missing the last 4 years Windows Updates. You need to install all missing/pending Windows updates.   After installing all updates, reboot your computer and try this:  Make sure that Skype is not running on your computer (Quit).   Download all files from this OneDrive site:   https://1drv.ms/f/s!AMmT0APdaW7bgU0   Open Skype installation folder:   C:Program Files (x86)SkypePhone   Paste all the downloaded files to this Skype installation folder.   Try now again to start Skype.
