title: LiveWeb Add-On in PowerPoint Requires 2nd Visit to Slide to Display
original_question: I've read through previous posts regarding LiveWeb and anomalies when using PowerPoint 2013/2016. I'll first say, I appreciate the work of Lisa Pillai for providing a solution that fills a gap of PowerPoint and a thank you to him for providing a free tool. It's been helpful to me with legacy versions of PowerPoint and works great. To be specific, the posts/threads/sites I have read through are: "LiveWeb in PowerPoint" - https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/msoffice/forum/msoffice_powerpoint-mso_winother/liveweb-in-powerpoint-2016/5c883360-cecc-4a67-8f11-b22be06a4db8 vanderBlog: Realtime Webpages - https://vdpluijm.blogspot.com/2014/01/real-time-webpages-in-powerpoint-2013.html LiveWeb FAQ's from Mr. Pillai - http://skp.mvps.org/livewebfaq.htm I have the add-in 'working' without errors for ActiveX or Macros by changing the respective registry entries for my Office 2016 .msi installation on one box and the ClickToRun installation on a 2nd box.  I have attempted a few different browser emulation modes after beginning with the defaults from the LiveWeb FAQ page. Here's the issue I am having: When I visit the slide with the LiveWeb control the very FIRST time the slide ONLY displays a blank page.  If I arrow back one page or use a hyperlink back to another page and then return to the LiveWeb slide I see the embedded webpage as desired.  Once I close the presentation and open it again I have to visit the page once (getting the blank page) then go to another slide and then come back in order  for the page to be displayed and continues to display until I close the presentation and open it again. Testing I have tested on two different machines and multiple profiles to attempt to eliminate any local issues.  I have used both the LiveWeb.exe install process and the ppam add-in steps during my testing. My configuration is: Win10 1703 (Creators Update) Office 2016 .msi  (although I have the same outcome on ClickToRun Machine) patched to July and then August 2017 with Office updates Default MS Edge Browser (I have also set IE as the default during testing) I have tested PowerPoint in Kiosk Mode (the one I'd like to use with the add-in) as well as standard "presented by speaker" mode. Both give same results. A thought or two: To add to what I am seeing, I can tell the add-in is running because when I run the presentation and hit the slide with LiveWeb control in it and it doesn't display I can "Alt-Tab" and see a process window running with the content.  I cannot open that window from there but it is there.  So, I know it is not being 'blocked' and I don't receive any errors.   Hopefully, I'm a little 'clearer than mudd' on explaining but glad to give more information if anyone out there has some thoughts one where I should focus my efforts. Thank you!
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I use the LiveWeb add-in in PowerPoint, the slide displays a blank page the very first time I visit it. I have to leave the slide and return to it a second time for the embedded webpage or file to actually display. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * **OS:** Windows 10 version 1703 (Creators Update) * **Software:** Office 2016 / PowerPoint (32-bit). Tested on both .msi installations (patched to July and August 2017) and ClickToRun installations. Also tested on Office 2016 Pro Plus. * **Add-in:** LiveWeb (tested using both the LiveWeb.exe install process and the .ppam add-in steps) * **Browser:** MS Edge (Default), also tested with Internet Explorer as the default. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * On the first visit to a slide with a LiveWeb control, it only displays a blank page. * If I arrow back or hyperlink to another page and then return to the LiveWeb slide, the embedded content displays perfectly. * Once I close and reopen the presentation, the issue resets, and I have to do the "visit, leave, and return" process all over again. * There are no error messages, and the content is not being blocked. * The issue occurs in both Kiosk Mode and standard "presented by speaker" mode. * When the slide is blank, pressing "Alt-Tab" reveals a process window running with the content, though I cannot open the window from there. * In Windows Task Manager, under "Microsoft PowerPoint (32 bit) (3)", there is a process with a "Window Icon" but no text. This represents the LiveWeb control. * If I right-click that Window Icon in Task Manager and choose "Bring to Front" during the first visit to the slide, the contents display immediately without needing a second visit. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Changed the respective registry entries to ensure the add-in works without ActiveX or Macro errors. * Attempted a few different browser emulation modes, starting with the defaults from the LiveWeb FAQ. * Tested on two different physical machines and multiple user profiles to rule out local issues. * Completely removed all LiveWeb components from PowerPoint and the OS, then tried a clean install with a blank presentation, but got the same result. * Set up two Virtual Machines (Win10 Pro 1607 and Win10 Education 1607) with Office 2016 Pro Plus. The add-in worked perfectly on the first slide visit on version 1607, even after applying all security patches prior to 1703. * Updated both VMs to the Windows 10 1703 Creators Update. After rebooting, the blank page issue immediately started occurring, confirming the 1703 update is the trigger. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am using the LiveWeb control heavily: it is embedded on 70 different slides to show individual PDF or PNG files. * Based on my Task Manager testing, I suspect the embedded control is simply losing top window focus on the first visit. * **Current Workaround:** I created a custom Slide Show containing only the slides with the embedded control. I let them cycle through once before my live presentation begins. I then stop the custom show (without exiting PowerPoint) and run the full show. Because the slides were already "visited" once, all the LiveWeb content displays perfectly during the actual presentation.
accepted_answer: Hi,  The behavior of the slide/live web has something to do with how it is inserted or placed in the PowerPoint slide. We suggest that you try to remove the live web and re-insert/re-add it on the slide.  Let us know what happens after doing the suggestion above.

title: PowerPoint 2016 for Mac frequently crashes when switching between Normal/Notes/Outline views
original_question: I'm on PowerPoint for Mac 2016, version 15.34 (apparently the latest version to date). I have noticed very frequent crashes when opening a blank presentation (or using an existing presentation) and then switching between Normal, Notes and Outline views. I do not have any font issues. I am running on OS X 10.11.6, 16 GB RAM. Wondering if anyone else is having similar troubles? I cannot get it to consistently crash, but switching between views, clicking occasionally on slides and switching between views will eventually crash the app. Switching between Notes and Normal view seems to be a frequent culprit. I also might add that I am using multiple monitors, and wonder if this has anything to do with the issue. - Amanda
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My PowerPoint 2016 for Mac frequently crashes when I switch between Normal, Notes, and Outline views. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - PowerPoint 2016 for Mac, version 15.34 (latest version via Office 365 subscription) - Primary machine: Mac OS X 10.11.6 (El Capitan), 16 GB RAM - Other affected machines: A second Mac running El Capitan, and a third Mac running macOS Sierra SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The app crashes when opening a blank or existing presentation and switching between Normal, Notes, and Outline views. - The crash isn't always immediate; clicking occasionally on slides and switching views will eventually crash the app. - Switching between Notes and Normal view is the most frequent culprit. - The crash specifically hinders safely switching views when there is text in the Notes pane/field. - The problem is not file-specific and happens with both blank and existing presentations. - I cannot share my specific presentation file due to confidentiality reasons, but I have provided a video of the crash via private message. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I confirmed that I do not have any font issues. - I applied the latest Office updates. - I tested PowerPoint 2011 for Mac, and the issue does not occur in that version. - I tested whether my multiple monitor setup was causing the issue, but the crash still occurs when using only a single monitor or when unplugged from an external display. - I successfully reproduced the exact same issue on three different Macs (two on El Capitan, one on Sierra) to confirm it is not isolated to my primary machine. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I am unsure if this issue occurred in previous versions of PowerPoint 2016.
accepted_answer: I wanted to add to this post that I experienced an identical problem as OP - After reading the solutions here, I discovered that the window management program "Magnet" from the apple store was the culprit in my case. I assume that these window management programs are using the same system resources and are causing the same error for MS powerpoint. Quitting Magnet seems to have resolved the problem. Just a note for future google-ers   Thanks all for the help

title: Login issues iPhone
original_question: Hi Skype, My iPhone automatically updated my (wonderful) old version 6.1.210 of Skype to the new (terrible) Skype. 30 minutes of wasted effort trying to work out how to use it I gave up and used my OS X desktop app. Luckily I had not updated my itunes app library so deleted the new version of Skype and reloaded the one I loved best back onto my phone. That's when my login in problems started. I have reset my password on the phone and have been able to log in using my MacBook Pro with it but it refuses to let me log in with my iPhone. When resetting the password via the phone it told me I needed to set up a Microsoft account which I did not want but did it anyway in the vague hope that it would let me log in. Sadly that did not work either. So at present my phone says "unable to connect to skype" and the only place I can log into Skype is via my desktop. So to recap:- Things I have tried but have not worked. I have checked my iPhone, computer and router network settings and can surf the web on my iPhone via wifi or 4G. I have reset the Skype password and created a Microsoft account but that does not help logging in on the phone. I have turned off the phone and on again. Went through the phone recover details process whereby it tells me I have one skype account but refuses to let me Sign in. Things I will not do. Use the new iOS version of Skype. Continue to have a Microsoft account as I don't want it or need it. I hope you can help me log in with my old version 6.1.210 please?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I restored an older, preferred version of Skype on my iPhone from a backup, but now the app refuses to let me log in. Because I refuse to use the new version of the app, I am now locked out on my phone and am requesting a full refund of my Skype credit so I can move to a different platform. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: iPhone * Secondary Device: MacBook Pro (OS X) * App: Skype for iOS * App Version: 6.1.210 (restored from an iTunes app library backup) * Network: Wi-Fi and 4G SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The iPhone app displays the exact error message: "unable to connect to skype". * The phone recovery details process recognizes that I have one Skype account, but still refuses to let me sign in. * The login issue started immediately after I deleted the new auto-updated version of Skype and reinstalled version 6.1.210. * I am completely unable to access my Skype credit on my mobile device. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Deleted the new version of Skype and reloaded version 6.1.210 from my iTunes app library. * Reset my Skype password via the phone. * Created a Microsoft account (which I was prompted to do during the password reset), even though I did not want one. * Checked my iPhone, computer, and router network settings; confirmed I can successfully surf the web on my iPhone via Wi-Fi and 4G. * Turned the iPhone off and on again. * Went through the phone recovery details process. * Successfully logged into the Skype OS X desktop app on my MacBook Pro using the reset password. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * My iPhone had accidentally auto-updated Skype to the newest version, which I found unusable. * I absolutely refuse to use the new iOS version of Skype and I refuse to keep the Microsoft account I was forced to create. * I suspect Skype/Microsoft intentionally implemented code (possibly around July 1st) to block older versions from logging in to force users to upgrade. * I want a full refund of my existing Skype credit. I read that because the automatic updates happened more than 14 days ago, my money might be stuck in the Skype credit system. * I am seeking confirmation on whether Skype is refusing to refund my credit, who legally owns the credit, and what my rights are regarding forced software changes and breach of contract.
accepted_answer: Hello Monica,     Thank you for your response. It may be reinstalled still, however, it will not be supported in the future. Having a retired version installed on your device may cause problems.   Please understand that this is done in good faith and to make way for better and improved Skype features.   Nonetheless, we respect your decision. For your refund request, kindly refer to this link: How do I request a refund?   Should you have further queries, please do not hesitate to reply to this post.   Thank you.

title: How do you remove a custom added Topic from bing news app?
original_question: Start at : Front page of the bing news app -   Right-click the screen over the feature story image > left-click the word Topics at top of screen > right-click screen > left-click Remove at bottom right  > left-click Topic to be removed > NOTHING HAPPENS!  No Topic can be removed.. only added.  I've added three custom topics and cannot remove any of them. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 Browser: Internet Explorer 11 (IE 11) App: Bing News app SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I attempt to remove a custom topic, nothing happens. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - From the front page: Right-clicked the screen over the feature story image > left-clicked "Topics" at the top > right-clicked the screen > left-clicked "Remove" at the bottom right > left-clicked the topic to be removed. - Opened the specific custom topic page (e.g., my local newspaper), right-clicked, and clicked "Remove" in the lower left. - Refreshed the app. - Closed and re-opened the app. - Followed the official help instructions (Customize > Topics > click red X), but my custom topics do not have a red X button. - Uninstalled and re-installed the Bing News app. This had no effect because the cloud saves my settings and reloads the custom topics automatically when I re-install. accepted_answer: Hi,  Open the Custom Topic - Ex:  You've added your local Newspaper. Note that this feature might not be available on every locale. title: Bing Video no longer works
original_question: Split from this thread. I thought it was just me.  In the last 3 or 4 days, I can do Bing searches for Web, Image, Maps, News, & Explore.  Videos no longer works.  I am re-directed to the default Bing search page with just a pretty picture of flying birds and random unrelated videos at the bottom of the page.  I though there was an upgrade or technical problem with Bing, and I'd wait a few days for them to fix it.  But the problem persists. BTW:  My browser is Firefox.  Explorer began seriously screwing up my PC two years ago, so I switched to Firefox.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I can no longer search for videos using Bing. Whenever I try to use Bing Video search, I get stuck in a redirect loop that sends me back to the default Bing homepage instead of showing video results. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Browser: Firefox (I stopped using Internet Explorer two years ago because it was causing PC issues) OS: Currently on an older version of Windows, but planning to upgrade to Windows 10 in a few days Security Software: Avast Antivirus, Windows Defender SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The issue started 3 or 4 days ago. Bing searches for Web, Image, Maps, News, and Explore work perfectly fine. When I click "videos", I am redirected to the default Bing search page, which just shows a background picture (flying birds) and random unrelated videos at the bottom. If I type a subject like "grumpy cat" on that default page, nothing happens. If I type "grumpy cat videos", it takes me to a different Bing search template showing regular web results. If I click "videos" from there, it loops me right back to the default Bing homepage. If I search "cat videos" from the web header and click one of the selections, I am again redirected back to the default Bing homepage. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I ran a disk scan at boot-up a few days ago. I ran a full virus scan with Avast, which found nothing. I checked Windows Defender, which also found nothing. I attempted some advanced troubleshooting steps but stopped because I was not comfortable and messed up the process. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have used Bing for videos thousands of times previously without any issues. My current workaround is to use Google to search for videos or go directly to YouTube from my bookmarks.
accepted_answer: Step 1) Start > Type Msconfig Select Boot tab Check Safe Boot Check Networking Click Apply, Ok Reboot and you'll enter Safe Mode with Networking Download the following tool and run a full scan http://www.microsoft.com/security/scanner/en-us/default.aspx  When finished with the scan, go back to Start > Msconfig Uncheck Safe Boot Click Apply, Ok Reboot and Windows will load normally.  Retry Videos  Step 2) If issue persists, clean boot the pc to determine if a third-party app is interfering.  Follow the steps here to clean boot. https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/kb/929135  Remember to reset the computer to restart normally when finished.   Check your add-ons and I'd advise contacting Firefox Support

title: adobe pdf viewer
original_question: Recently, I updated Adobe Reader on my Mac. When using Excel or Word now, I click preview (print preview) and it automatically goes to Adobe Reader's preview and changes my file to a PDF. I like using Preview in the Office for Mac WITHOUT the Adobe Reader hijacking it. Even when I quit Adobe Reader, it automatically opens when I click Preview. Any way to change the settings to not allow Adobe Reader to hijack my preview?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I click print preview in Excel or Word, the system bypasses the default Office preview, automatically launches Adobe Reader, and converts my file to a PDF. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Mac * OS: Mac OS X * Software: Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Word (Office for Mac), Adobe Reader SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Clicking "preview" (print preview) in Excel or Word automatically opens Adobe Reader. * The process changes my file to a PDF and adds the .pdf extension to it. * Even if I completely quit Adobe Reader beforehand, it still automatically launches when I click preview. * The Adobe Reader preview is not as accurate as the native Office preview. * Printed documents do not look the same because they are now printing from the generated Adobe PDF file instead of the original Excel file. * The issue started today; the native preview was working perfectly yesterday. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I tried quitting Adobe Reader before previewing, but it forces itself open anyway. * I highlighted an Excel file and used the Finder > File > Get Info command. It correctly shows Microsoft Excel as the application and shows XLS in the preview area. (Initially, I did this without highlighting the file and just saw a picture of a house). * I searched through the settings in both Excel and Adobe Reader to try and stop this behavior, but I could not figure out how to change it. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I recently updated Adobe Reader, which is exactly when this behavior started. * I did not update Excel or Word, nor did I intentionally change any default settings. * I am not an advanced user and simply want to restore the standard Mac OS X default preview.
accepted_answer: Choosing an XL file won't work - when you choose XL's FIle/Print Preview, you're NOT looking at an XL file any more, you're looking at the output of OSX's Print to PDF, in whatever viewer you have chosen as the default.  Note that if you want Adobe Reader to be your default PDF viewer, you'll have to live with Print Preview using Reader, or else choose File/Print, and click the PDF button to choose Open PDF in XXX....

simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I add a folder or album of photos to the Windows 10 Photos App via settings, the app drops random photos and does not import or display all of the pictures from the selected folder. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO I am using a Windows 10 device (referred to as both a computer and a tablet). My Windows 10 operating system is completely up to date with all available updates. accepted_answer: Hello, If issue persists, I suggest you to uninstall and reinstall the Photo app. Here's how: 1. Press Start, and type Powershell.  2. From results, right click on Windows PowerShell and select Run as administrator option.  3. Type Get-AppxPackage and press Enter. (This command will output a long list of all installed apps containing lots of information about each app).  4. Type Get-AppxPackage | Select Name, PackageFullName and press Enter. (This command will show only the name of the app and its PackageFullName information).  5. Select the PackageFullName information of the desired app and copy it.  6. Type Get-AppxPackage PackageFullName | Remove-AppxPackage and press Enter. (This command is to remove the app from the system, replace PackageFullName with the copied information with Step 5).  For example, if you want to remove Photo app, the command to remove it will be as following:  7. Wait till completion of the command, and check if the app is uninstalled, if not restart the system and check. If you want to uninstall the app from all user accounts, use the following command format:  If you want to remove the app from any particular user account, use following command: (Replace username in the above command with the correct user account name).  8. Launch the Windows Store and reinstall the Photo app.  Hope this helps. If the issue remains unresolved, please get back to us and we would be happy to help.

title: Bing Desktop has stopped working
original_question: The app loads, and is visible on my desktop, but none of the pulldown menus work, and it does not refresh my desktop picture. Opening System Information, I can see a message that says something to effect of: "Bing Desktop stopped communicating and was closed, check Action Center for more possible solution". However, when I check Action Center, it doesn't show anything about Bing Desktop, and actually doesn't show anything about anything. There are no archived messages at all.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM Bing Desktop loads and is visible on my screen, but it is completely unresponsive and does not refresh my desktop picture. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 (x64 system) Software: Bing Desktop, Bing Bar Browser: Internet Explorer 11 Security: Antivirus and malware removal tools installed Updates: Automatic updates enabled SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The Bing Desktop app is visible on my desktop, but none of the pulldown menus work. The circled "i" that used to be on the top right of the desktop is missing. System Information shows an error message: "Bing Desktop stopped communicating and was closed, check Action Center for more possible solution". The Action Center is completely empty and shows no archived messages. The Bing Bar has the exact same issue: it is visible, but the icons and pulldown menus do nothing except become highlighted when pressed. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I checked the Action Center for solutions, but there was nothing there. I installed two optional Windows updates (one for Bing on IE 11, and one for x64 systems). I performed a clean uninstall and reinstall of Bing Desktop, verifying that no leftover registry data or hidden folders remained. I ran my antivirus and malware tools, which show my PC is clean. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I recently deleted my Facebook account and disconnected Facebook from my Microsoft account. Bing Desktop stopped working around this same time.
accepted_answer: My apologies:  To remove all traces, reboot and reinstall the application  1. Uninstall the Bing Desktop program and earlier versions a. Click Start. b. Type appwiz.cpl in the Search for programs and files box and then press ENTER. c. In the currently installed programs list, select Bing Desktop and then click Remove. d. Follow the on-screen instructions to uninstall Bing Desktop from your computer.   NOTE: Before proceeding to the next steps, please unhide your folders first. To unhide folders, on your local folder, press ALT > Tools > Folder Option > View > Show hidden files, folders and drives.   2. Browse your computer to C:Program Files (x86) then delete the Bing Desktop Installer. a. Click Start. b. Type C:Program Files on the Start Search box. c. Hit Enter. d. Look for the Bing Desktop folder. e. Right-click the Bing Desktop Installer folder and click Delete   3. Remove Bing Desktop Files in Program Data folder a. Click Start b. Be sure to unhide all your folders c. Type 'C:' d. Select Program Data e. Then, open Microsoft f. Delete the Bing Desktop folder   4. Remove Bing Desktop files from the Windows registry IMPORTANT: The steps below contain information about modifying the registry. Before you modify the registry, make sure to back it up and make sure that you understand how to restore the registry if a problem occurs. For information about how to back up, restore, and edit the registry, visit the following Web site: http://support.microsoft.com/kb/256986 a. Click the 'Start' button, type regedit in the search text box, and then press 'ENTER'. b. In the 'User Account Control' dialog box, click 'Continue'. c. Expand the HKEY_CURRENT_USERSoftwareMicrosoft folder. d. Underneath the Microsoft folder, make sure that the MSN Toolbar, MSN Apps and Bing bar folders are not present. If the folders are present, right-click on each folder then click 'Delete'. e. Expand the HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE SoftwareMicrosoft folder. f. Underneath the Microsoft folder, make sure that the MSN Toolbar and MSN Apps folders are not present. If the folders are present, right-click on each folder then click 'Delete'. g. Close the Registry Editor window   5. To delete the Bing the APP Data folders a. Click Start. Type %USERPROFILE% on the Search for programs and files box then press Enter on your keyboard. b. On the pop-up window, locate the AppData folder. If the folder is not present, make sure to show all hidden files and folders. Check this option by accessing Organize > Folder and Search options > View Tab c. After opening the AppData folder, open Local > Microsoft d. Locate the Bing Desktop folder then delete it.   6. Install the Bing Desktop a. Launch Internet Explorer. The Bing Bar will not work with any other browser. b. Download the installer by going to  http://www.bing.com/explore/desktopto install the latest version of the Bing Bar. c. Restart the computer then check if the issue persists

title: Legacy v UEFI - Dell E6420 BIOS
original_question: Hi There  the Dell E6420 Laptop I have is at the Legacy not UEFI set up.  I have read that by resetting it to UEFI I might be able to increase the HDD capacity to 2TB.  However, I would like to know how this would affect the machine in booting and performance. When I changed it to  UEFI to see, I found the boot sequences that were ticked for Legacy were grayed out as if no more applicable.  So, please advise what are the pros & cons if changed to UEFI and if that is necessary at all. The machine as of now, is working fine. Thanks.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I want to change my laptop's BIOS from Legacy to UEFI so I can upgrade my hard drive capacity, but I need to know the correct sequence of events because simply switching the mode causes the system to fail to boot. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: Dell E6420 Laptop - Operating System: Windows 10 Pro - Current BIOS Mode: Legacy - Current Storage: 500GB HDD - Desired Storage: 1TB or up to 2TB HDD - Installation Media: Windows 10 MCT (Media Creation Tool) on a USB stick - Installed Software: MS Office, various AV (Audio/Visual) programs SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - When I temporarily switched the BIOS to UEFI, the boot sequences that were ticked for Legacy were grayed out and no longer applicable. - Leaving it in UEFI mode resulted in a boot failure. - The machine currently works perfectly fine as long as it remains in Legacy mode. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I changed the BIOS to UEFI to test it, saw the grayed-out boot sequences and experienced a boot failure, so I backed off and reverted the settings. - I previously upgraded to Windows 10 Pro and did a couple of reinstalls, but missed the opportunity to switch to UEFI during those times. - I have mapped out a potential upgrade plan: reset BIOS to UEFI, shut down, connect a new larger HDD, install Windows 10 Pro using a USB stick (MCT), and then reinstall all my software. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I am a layman regarding technical details. - My biggest hesitation with this upgrade is the tedious process of having to manually reinstall all my software (like MS Office and my AV tools) one by one.
accepted_answer: Do you already have an Operating system installed in Legacy mode? If so switching to EUFI mode will stop the OS from booting. You will have to have a EUFI bootable drive to install an OS like Windows 10. Yes the benefits are that 2TB and bigger partitions are supported as well as supporting fast boot. EUFI is faster and more efficient than Legacy. Hope that helps!

title: Worksheet borders
original_question: This relates to Excel 2016 and pdf pages. For some unknown reason, I cannot go to the edge of my worksheet and find the arrow symbol that allows me to drag the border away from the edge of the page. I am trying to reduce the size of my view so I can view more than one program image at the same time. Never had this problem before in any Microsoft program.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I cannot resize my application windows to occupy only part of my screen, which prevents me from viewing multiple programs side-by-side. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Excel 2016, PDF viewer Device: Laptop SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS My Excel worksheets and other programs currently fill the entire screen. When I move my cursor to the edge of the window, the horizontal arrow symbol that normally allows me to drag and shrink the border does not appear. Additionally, the restore/maximize icons do not toggle or change appearance when clicked, regardless of the program. They appear to be completely locked in the "restore/toggle down" position. Because I cannot resize windows, I am forced to completely minimize one program just to view another. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried moving my cursor to the edge of the Excel window to drag the right-hand edge to the middle of the screen. I also tried to downsize a .pdf document, but the exact same issue occurred. Finally, I tried repeatedly clicking the restore/maximize icons to toggle the window size, but they remain unresponsive. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have never experienced this problem before in any Microsoft program. My computer recently installed an update that changed a lot of things, and this issue started occurring right after that.
accepted_answer: You can only resize a window that is not maximized.  In the top right corner of every window, regardless of what application it is, you see icons to minimize, maximize/restore and the X for exit. When a window is maximized, it cannot be resized. You need to click the maximize/restore icon to restore the window. It is a toggle switch. The first screenshot shows what it looks like in a maximized window that takes up the whole screen. The second screenshot shows what it looks like if the window is not maximized. This window's borders can be dragged.     Another quick way in Windows 7 and higher to see windows side by side is to select one window, hold down the Window key on your keyboard and hit the right arrow. That will send the window to the right hand side of the screen and it will take up half of the monitor. In Windows 10 you now can click any of the other open windows and it will be arranged on the left hand side of the screen. In older Windows versions you need to repeat the step to select a window and use the Windows key with the left arrow.

title: can't open excel 2002 file format on mac office 365 app.
original_question: I got a business excel report daily, previously I use windows office 2016 and all working normal, but after I use macbook, and also use office 2016(corporate licensed), the excel file can't be open with a note in the blank sheet "This page uses frames, but your browser doesn't support them." I was noted by the report owner that the file format is excel2002, should be compatible with higher office app than 2003, but that's the problem. please try to figure out if there any hotfix or update for office365 which can fix this issue for me?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I receive a daily business Excel report that I cannot open on my MacBook. When I try to open the file using the Mac desktop version of Excel, it fails to load the content and instead displays a blank sheet with an error message. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: MacBook * Software: Office 2016 / Office 365 (Corporate licensed), full desktop version of Excel * Language: Chinese version of Excel * Previous/Alternative Setup: Windows OS workstation with Windows Office 2016 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Exact error message displayed in the blank sheet: "This page uses frames, but your browser doesn't support them." * The file format is Excel 2002 (.xls / .xlst). * When opened, the file name displayed in Excel is incorrect. It shows up as a newly created file (e.g., "workbook1" or "workbook2") instead of the specific name I saved it as on my hard drive. * The report is generated and exported by IBM Cognos reporting. * The report generates on a website and displays normally in HTML format online before I export it. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I exported and downloaded the file directly to my local hard drive and tried opening it with the full desktop version of Excel for Mac. * I sent the exact same downloaded file to another workstation running Windows OS and Office 2016, and it opened and displayed the content perfectly. * I checked my license and version information in "About Excel" and took screenshots of both the version info and the error message. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * Everything worked normally before I switched to a MacBook. * I cannot share the actual file with support because it is a confidential business report.
accepted_answer: Neither the creator version nor the file type is the issue. The problem is that the file contains Frames which are ActiveX Controls. ActiveX Controls are not supported by Excel Online [which the "browser" message suggests you're actually trying to use] or by any version of Excel for Mac.  It further sounds like the file may be stored on OneDrive or other cloud service. I don't have any way to test, but unless there is other unsupported content I believe the Online app should be able to open the file, but the unsupported content either will not be displayed or will not be usable.  If you download the file to your local HD you should be able to open it with the full desktop version of Excel, but the ActiveX Controls still will not be functional.  Verify that your installation of Office is fully updated by going to Excel> About Excel in the main menu bar of your locally installed software. The current version is 15.38 or later. If you continue to have a problem please include the Version & License information in your reply along with the exact wording (or screen shots) of any messages as well as any additional specific details you can supply.

title: hp printer doesn't print anymore
original_question: I use Windows 7. My HP P1102w printer assigned to a USB port has been printing for a couple of years.  It suddenly stopped. I have tried deleting and uninstalling the software and reinstalling it from the HP site three times without success.  I followed all of the instructions regarding when to plug in the power and USB cables.   The power comes on.  The printer shows in Devices and Printers.   Troubleshooting does not reveal a problem. I have checked the HP site and could not find a solution.   I used HP Print and Scan Doctor.  Nothing comes up. I used Malware Bytes to see if there was anything.  Nothing. I used Wise Registry Cleaner to see if it would clean superfluous registry entries.  No change. I checked the cable connections multiple times and even changed USB ports.  Nada. Anyone have any thoughts about this? Thank you! Daniel
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My HP LaserJet P1102w printer suddenly stopped printing after working perfectly for a couple of years. There is absolutely no response from the printer when I attempt to print. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Operating System: Windows 7 Hardware: HP LaserJet P1102w printer Connection Type: USB SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The printer powers on normally. The printer shows up in "Devices and Printers". Windows Troubleshooting does not detect any problems. When looking at the print queue and Devices and Printers, there are three HP LaserJet P1102w printers listed: - One is ghosted with an exclamation point. - One is clear (not ghosted) with an exclamation point. - One is listed as "Copy 3". There are unprinted documents sitting in the print queues for the two non-ghosted printers. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I deleted, uninstalled, and reinstalled the printer software from the HP website three times. I carefully followed all instructions regarding exactly when to plug in the power and USB cables during the software installation. I searched the HP website for a solution. I ran HP Print and Scan Doctor, but it found nothing. I ran MalwareBytes to check for malware, but found nothing. I ran Wise Registry Cleaner to clean superfluous registry entries, which resulted in no change. I checked the cable connections multiple times and tried plugging the printer into different USB ports. I took the USB printer cable I was using for the P1102w and tested it on another printer I have connected; the cable worked perfectly and the other printer printed. I checked the print queues multiple times. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have at least one other working printer connected to this same computer.
accepted_answer: Congratulations!  You'll probably want to leave things alone, but in case you don't ....  This (https://h30434.www3.hp.com/t5/LaserJet-Printing/Why-are-there-two-icons-for-my-P1102w-in-Devices-and/td-p/2157411) may explain the second (ghosted icon).  Or it may not.  Also see post #4 here: https://www.sevenforums.com/drivers/229868-ghost-printer-devices-printers-i-can-not-remove.html  HP's "Smart Install" was a feature built into the firmware of some printers, including the P1102w).  If you connected a printer with "Smart Install" by a USB cable to an Internet-connected computer, the computer would automatically go to HP's servers, find, download, and install the correct driver.  HP disabled the servers that supported Smart Install ... but apparently printers with this feature sometimes keep trying anyway unless Smart Install is disabled.  See the first article above for details.  If you don't have the CD that came with the printer, the Smart Install Control Utility is still downloadable from: https://support.hp.com/us-en/drivers/selfservice/swdetails/hp-laserjet-pro-p1102-printer-series/4110394/model/4110303/swItemId/lj-129224-1  If you get bothered by looking at "Copy 3" as part of the printer's name, you can right-click on the icon in Devices and Printers, select "Printer properties" and rename it on the "General" tab.  It can't be the same as the name used by the "ghost printer" so if you don't remove that one, you'll have to give it some other name.

original_question: I want to create the effect of a slow drip using animation in PowerPoint. I need it to repeat until I advance to the next slide. What I can't figure out is how to have that animation repeat until I click away from the slide. The "Repeat" function is only applied to individual animations, not to the entire set of animations as a whole. How can I get the fade in/fade out fade in/fade out fade in/fade out etc. effect to continue repeating itself on a loop from the beginning? Counting on you geniuses to be way more clever than I am. Many thanks!
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I want to create a slow drip animation effect in PowerPoint that repeats continuously until I advance to the next slide, but I cannot figure out how to loop an entire sequence of animations as a whole. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: PowerPoint SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS I have five droplets evenly spaced in a vertical line. Each droplet fades in and then out, in order, making it look like the same drop is slowly moving down the line. The built-in "Repeat" function only applies to individual animations, not to the entire set of animations. I cannot get the full sequence of fade in/fade out effects to continue repeating itself on a loop from the beginning until I click away from the slide. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I attempted to use the "Repeat" function, but found it only loops individual animations, not the entire group. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My slide design has text inside a shape. I cannot use a single sliding drop animation because the drop slides behind the text, which is why I specifically need the five individual drops to work. My network firewall blocks downloads from external sites, so I cannot easily download external files or tools to test without finding a less restricted computer, which will take me a few days.
accepted_answer: The only way to do this as you want is to construct your own custom custom animation. This is clearly not a trivial task.  If you download this demo I have created the five animations and you MIGHT be able to use the animation painter to apply each animation to the drops in turn.  The animation is specific to having exactly five drops! Do not try to change the timings.

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: New computer (only 2 weeks old) * Browsers: Opera 37 and Firefox 46 (both are the latest versions and were installed new, not copied over via file transfer) * Search Engine: Bing is set as the default search engine on both browsers * Account: Signed in to my Microsoft account on both browsers SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Bing search looks completely different in Opera compared to Firefox. * All filters in the Image search results are missing when using Opera. * The Bing Rewards amount displayed is also different between the two browsers. * I have a side-by-side screenshot of the exact same Bing search in both Firefox and Opera demonstrating the missing filters. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Cleaned out the caches in both browsers. * Experimented with substituting the "User Agent" in the browsers using Add-Ons. * Tested several User Agent variations to see how Bing responds. title: Size explosion when changing brightness and temperature of picture slides
original_question: I am stunned to find that the file size becomes about 10+ times as big after changing just brightness and temperature (hue) of simple picture slides using macro.  For instance a 70 MB file becomes 800 MB.  The file had about 150 slides. Will the same kind of size explosion take place if brightness and temperature are changed one by one manually via Picture Format pane instead of running macro, too?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My PowerPoint presentation file sizes increase by more than 10 times their original size when I adjust the brightness or temperature (hue) of picture slides. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: PowerPoint SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When adjusting the brightness or temperature of simple picture slides, the file size explodes. In my main file containing about 150 slides, the size jumped from 70 MB to 800 MB. This size explosion happens regardless of whether the image adjustments are made manually via the Picture Format pane or automatically using a VB macro. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I conducted a series of tests using a new 5-slide presentation (originally 247 KB) to isolate the cause: - Test 1: Manually increased picture brightness by 9% via the Format Picture pane. The file size became 2,844 KB. - Test 2: Manually reduced temperature (hue) by 1,000 (from 6,500 to 5,500). The file size became 2,864 KB. - Test 3: Manually changed both brightness and temperature together. The file size became 2,836 KB. - Test 4: Used a VB macro to change the brightness of all slides. The file size became 2,843 KB. These tests confirmed the VB macro is not the root cause of the size explosion. I also tried using the "Compress Pictures" tool, but it did not help and the file size remained unchanged. When attempting this, the resolution setting was locked to "Use default resolution" and was the only selectable option. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I am working with PNG pictures. I am not a VB developer.
accepted_answer: Try  Select a pic Format in Picture tools > Compress Pictures  UNTICK Apply to This picture Only  Set to 96 dpi or 150 dpi  OK

title: "script error - not implemented": when connecting Facebook to Bing Bar
original_question: I've tried uninstalling then reinstalling the Bing Bar, and I get the same error message when I click the Facebook icon on Bing bar. When the "Bing wants to access your friends, etc..." comes up, I don't get the option to "allow" and an error message comes up "script error - not implemented". Bing bar is activated in the add ons, and I'm using Windows 7. Also, Bing bar seems to be slowing things down just in general. Oh, and when I go to "connect bing to facebook" it doesn't load my info but brings me to mountain biking. How strange is that? Regular updates are run, and I clean out old files etc. regularly. Thank you !
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to connect my Facebook account to the Bing Bar, but I cannot log in or grant permissions due to a recurring script error and a missing "allow" button on the authorization screen. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Operating System: Windows 7 - Software: Bing Bar (activated and enabled in add-ons) - Integration: Facebook SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - When I click the Facebook icon (located to the left of the search on the Bing Bar), a prompt appears saying "Bing wants to access your friends, etc..." (or "Bing would like access..."). - An error message immediately pops up stating: "script error - not implemented". - The access prompt does not display an "allow" or "okay" button anywhere on the screen. - If I click "no" on the script error, it loops back to the "Bing would like access..." screen, still with no option to approve. - If I press "Enter", it opens the very long Microsoft Services Agreement, which also has no option to allow or enter at the bottom. - When I use the specific "connect bing to facebook" option, it does not load my information and bizarrely redirects me to a page about mountain biking. - The Bing Bar seems to be slowing down my system in general. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Uninstalled and reinstalled the Bing Bar. - Verified that the Bing Bar is enabled in my browser add-ons. - Tried making the screen/text smaller, thinking the "okay" button might be pushed off the bottom of the screen, but it did not reveal any hidden buttons. - Tried numerous keyboard shortcut keys to bypass the screen or accept the prompt, but nothing happens. - I regularly run system updates and clean out old files to keep my computer maintained.
accepted_answer: Hi Patricia,   I am happy to assist you with your issue in connecting your Facebook account to Bing Bar.   We recommend that you try to optimize your browser first by clearing its cache and cookies before connecting your Facebook account to Bing Bar to remove any memory clutters that may affect your browser. To do so, you may follow the steps below:     1. Launch Internet Explorer   2. Press Ctrl + Shift + Delete buttons on your keyboard simultaneously.   3. Make sure to check the following items on the list: "Preserve Favorites website data", "Temporary Internet files", "Cookies" and "History"   4. Click “Delete” button.   5. Close all browsers and re-launch your browser.   After doing the steps above, retry connecting your Facebook from Bing Bar, you may follow the steps below:   1.     Click on Facebook icon on Bing Bar         2.     Enter your username and password and click on Login                3.     The page stating "Bing would like to post publicly on your behalf" will appear next. Click "No" and then the "Okay" or "Skip" buttons to proceed.          4.     The following page will appear next, "Bing would like to manage your messages and manage your notifications." Like the previous steps, you must stop running the script on the page by clicking the "No" button. You may click on "Okay" or "Skip" buttons to proceed.           Your Facebook account should now be connected to Bing Bar. If the steps helped you, please come back and let us know, if not, we will be happy to help you more.   Thanks!

title: Anyone know a work around a MSWord 2016 problem?
original_question: A huge part of my work involves putting together MSWord documents with tons of hyper-linked lists on each page. Word is supposed to allow one to setup as default for all links on a page or document “open links” in a new page as the target frame when a link is clicked. Which is supposed to append " 	 "_blank" at the end of a link. but there is a bug, that feature doesn't work. It works for setting up one link at a time, but if you’re working with several hundred links on a page, setting one link at a time is a huge time waste or downright unfeasible.  Is there a registry or some other fix for such a problem? Or does anyone know of an HTML editor that would allow as default "open all links in a new window"? Thanks!
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need to set a default in my documents so that all hyperlinks open in a new page as the target frame, but the feature that applies this default to an entire page or document does not work. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Word 2016 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The default setting is supposed to automatically append " \t "_blank" " to the end of every link, but it fails to do so due to a bug. The feature only works when setting up the target frame for one link at a time. Because I work with several hundred links on a single page, setting them individually is a huge waste of time and downright unfeasible. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I have manually set the target frame for individual links one at a time (which successfully appends the _blank switch), but I cannot get it to apply as a default to all links. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT A huge part of my work involves putting together documents with tons of hyper-linked lists on each page. I am looking for a registry fix, another workaround, or recommendations for an HTML editor that defaults to opening all links in a new window.
accepted_answer: You could run a macro containing the following code to add 	 _blank to each hyperlink that did not already have it  Dim i As Long, j As Long With ActiveDocument     For i = 1 To .Fields.Count         With .Fields(i)             If .Type = wdFieldHyperlink Then                 If InStr(.Code.Text, "	 _blank") = 0 Then                     .Code.Text = Code.Text & " 	 _blank"                 End If             End If         End With     Next i End With Note, I am not sure whether the switch should be:  	 "_blank"  or just  	 _blank  but you can modify the code as appropriate.

title: Received junk email from my own email address
original_question: I'm using MSN on Windows 10 and I received junk email from my own email address. sample below, I get these by the hour 30-40 daily "Do you like sex with asian girls less than 25 years old? They're waiting for you" How can i stop this  as I cant block my own address..? Moved from: Windows / Windows 10 / Security & privacy / PC
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am receiving a massive amount of inappropriate, pornographic junk email that appears to be sent from my own email address, which makes it impossible for me to block the sender. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Operating System: Windows 10 - Software/Service: MSN / Outlook Email - Device: PC SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - I receive spam emails with highly inappropriate subject lines and messages (e.g., "Do you like sex with asian girls less than 25 years old? They're waiting for you", "(Don't feel like you can fully satisfy your partner?) Russian or Asians Girls to Fxxck)"). - The emails show my own email address as the sender. - The emails arrive by the hour. - The volume started at 30-40 emails daily, but has escalated to as many as 87 in a single day (including a batch of 29 in one morning). - None of these junk emails appear in my "Sent" folder. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I ran both virus and malware scans, but no threats were detected. - I checked my account activity page and saw an unsuccessful sign-in attempt from China today. - I secured my account by changing my password and updating my profile. - I blocked and reported emails from a great number of these sites, but the issue persists because I cannot block my own email address. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - My IP address is correct. - I have had this email address for a very long time and I absolutely do not want to change it to fix this issue.
accepted_answer: Hi Shelly,  It seems likely that either your computer or your email account is infested with a malware. If this is the case, we suggest that you run a full virus / malware scan on your computer. Also, check your Sent folder to make sure the junk messages aren't there. If the said junk mail is there, that's a sign that your account was compromised. You can also check your Recent Activity page (https://account.live.com/Activity) to make sure all successful connections come from your own ISP's address space. In addition to this, enable a two-step verification on your email account.  You may also try changing your email address, then check if you'll still receive the same junk mail.  Let us know the outcome.

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Dell Latitude 3460 * OS: Windows 10 Pro, version 1607 (OS Build 14393.1715) * Processor: Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-5200U CPU @ 2.20GHz * RAM: 12.0GB * Storage: Samsung SSD 850 Evo 250G * Modem: Huawei E153 3G modem * Conflicting Software: Panasonic TAPI (panasonic_tsp_instx64_4212.exe) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When the modem refuses to connect, I get the exact error message: "After starting, the service hung in a start-pending state." * The RASMAN service gets stuck in a starting state. * The Network and Sharing page freezes completely. It takes 5-10 minutes just for the settings window to load, and then another 5-10 minutes of waiting before I can access the adapter settings screen. * The Windows update to version 1703 is currently failing to install (I am unsure if this is related). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I searched the error message and identified that the RASMAN service was failing to start. * I tried going into my network connections to change the IP of my ethernet port, which is when I discovered the severe freezing/loading delays in the settings menus. * I checked for outstanding Windows updates. * I isolated the root cause of the RASMAN hang to the installation of the Panasonic TAPI software. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * My Wi-Fi works perfectly fine and I can access the internet without any issues using it. * I absolutely must have the Panasonic TAPI software running to support a legacy software project. * My current workaround is that I have to completely uninstall the Panasonic TSP software and restart my laptop every single time I want to connect my 3G modem.
accepted_answer: We appreciate your response. Your concern about connectivity issues can be due to software conflicts related to the specified program that you're using. The specified application installs USB drivers and lists a number of items to consider when using USB as your preferred private branch exchange (PBX) setting. This may have brought changes to the configuration of the USB ports that led to conflicts on your device.  We'd recommend performing a clean boot in Windows to isolate software conflicts on your device. To do this, you can perform the steps posted on this link. In the clean boot environment, check if you'll still experience connectivity issues when using the application along with the 3G dongle. The link will also provide steps to isolate the possible cause of the issue. Once you're done troubleshooting, check the How to reset the computer to start normally after clean boot troubleshooting section to revert your device to normal boot.  In the event that you'll still experience your concern, we'd recommend performing the following to assist in identifying application specific issues:  We look forward to an update on your concern.

title: Word 2016: When a column is created with multiple rows and a single row is split into multiple columns, the text from the remaining columns disappears
original_question: I have tested this in Word 2013 and Word 2016, both versions have the latest updates.  Word 2013 was tested on Windows 7 x64 Enterprise and Word 2016 was tested on Windows 10 Enterprise. In word 2016, When a column is created with multiple rows and a single row is split into multiple columns, the text from the remaining columns disappears. Example Presplit table, the test word is displayed correctly: Column 4 is split into 2 rows, the remaining columns do not display the word Test: The same test in Word 2013 displays the text correctly: In Word 2016 when the text is not displayed you cannot select the blank cells at all, I have tried a few different ways of wrapping the text but nothing works.  Have tried text wrapping: around and displaying the text centrally which do not display the text.  Any ideas? Has anyone else seen this problem?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I create a table with multiple rows and split a single row into multiple columns, the text from the remaining columns completely disappears. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Microsoft Word 2016 (with the latest updates) running on Windows 10 Enterprise - Also tested on Microsoft Word 2013 (with the latest updates) running on Windows 7 x64 Enterprise - No add-ons are loaded in my Word application SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - In a pre-split table, text displays correctly. - When I split a cell (for example, splitting the 4th row/column 4 into 2 rows), the text in the remaining columns (columns 1-3) vanishes. - Once the text disappears, I am completely unable to click on or select those blank cells at all. - The issue is specifically triggered by splitting a column into another row, and has nothing to do with merging cells. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I tried different text wrapping settings, including wrapping "around" and displaying the text centrally, but the text still will not display. - I loaded Word in Safe Mode and created the table from scratch in a new document, but the exact same issue occurred. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - This issue is isolated to Word 2016; the exact same table test works perfectly in Word 2013. - I suspect this problem was caused by a recent Windows update.
accepted_answer: Sorry, I am at a loss. Your problems are definitely not the way Word is supposed to work. Normally, this sort of strange problem is caused by problems with Addins. From here the normal trouble shooting process is: - do an Office Repair, - uninstall Office from Control Panel > Programs and Features, - uninstall Office using Fix It tool You test to see if problem is fixed between steps. The first step just "refreshes" your program code. There should be no impact on your setup The second step re-installs Office while keeping all of your current customizations, macros etc. Again, no impact. The third step also wipes out all of your customizations and re-installs. Sometimes problems are located in the customizations. Good luck

title: I have this bing bar platform in my control panel & do not know how to remove it?
original_question: I  have no idea as to where this..bing bar platform got in my control panel under programs & it will not let me click on it to delete it? any ideas?  thanks
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have a program called Bing Bar listed in my Control Panel, and I cannot remove or delete it. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Bing Bar OS: Windows (using Control Panel) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Bing Bar is listed in my Control Panel under programs. When I click on it, the standard options to "uninstall", "change", or "repair" do not appear at all. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched my computer for Bing Bar and removed the files I found, but the program is still listed in my Control Panel. I tried clicking on it in the programs list to uninstall it, but the uninstall option will not show up. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I know how to uninstall programs normally, but this specific program won't let me. I did not install Bing Bar myself and have no idea how it got onto my computer in the first place.
accepted_answer: Hi WendyMcDonald,   Thank you for coming to Microsoft Community today. We’ll do our best to help you out.   In order to ensure that Bing Bar Uninstall component are still intact and not corrupted, we suggest that you try to reinstall Bing Bar. You may follow the steps below (steps below may apply to Windows Vista and 7):   1.     Reboot your computer to make sure that all Internet Explorer are closed.   2.     Download the Bing Bar installer here and save it on your Desktop   3.     After download finishes, double click on installer and follow the instructions   4.     Reboot again your PC   5.     Try uninstalling Bing Bar by going to Control Panel > Programs and Features   6.     Select Bing Bar on the list and click on Uninstall   If Bing Bar is still present on the list after doing the steps above, you may need to manually remove some files and folders left by Bing Bar on the following locations (steps below may apply to Windows Vista and 7):   Remove Bing Bar folder from Program Files   1.     Click on Start 2.     Type C:Program Files on the Search programs and files box and press Enter Note: If you are running on a 64-bit OS, the location is on C:Program Files (x86)   3.     Double click on Microsoft Folder   4.     Right-click on Bing Bar Folder and click Delete   Remove Bing Bar folder from AppData   1.     Click on Start 2.     Type %userprofile% on the Search programs and files box and press Enter   3.     On the pop-up window, locate and double-click on AppData folder. If the folder is not present, you may need to show hidden files, to do this follow the instruction on this link.   4.     Double click on Microsoft folder   5.     Right-click on Bing Bar Folder and click Delete   After deleting the folders, restart your computer then check if Bing Bar is still present on Programs and Features list.   Let us know if the steps provided works or not so we could assist you even further.   Thanks!

accepted_answer: Your post is not clear. I will answer assuming that you meant to say:  Hello, I would like to know if it's true that hotmail addresses will be phased out at some point in time. I have seen some post online but I haven't seen an official post from microsoft explaining the same. And if it's true, how do I avoid the impacts of losing my email address, is it true that i can actually upgrade to an outlook.com address without having to create another account? Thank you in advance  New Hotmail.com addresses can still be created when creating a new account. You cannot create a new Hotmail.com alias address. You can add a new @outlook.com address to your account as an alias.  Login to your Hotmail account. Click the Gear icon > Connected accounts > Manage or choose a primary alias > Enter password > Add alias

title: Screen Flicker
original_question: My screen flickers on my Acer laptop when I log in as a standard user.  I do not have this problem when I log in with the admin account on the laptop.  I did replace the laptop screen.  I am not sure if this is causing the problem.  Any suggestions on solving the problem.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My screen flickers when I log in to my laptop using a standard user account. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 10 Device: Acer V3-575T laptop Display Adapter: Intel(R) HD Graphics 520 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The screen flickers continuously when I am logged in as a standard user. The issue does not occur at all when I log in using the admin account. When I try to run commands while logged into the admin account, I receive an error stating that I need admin privileges to run them. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I recently replaced the laptop screen, though I am not sure if this caused the issue. I uninstalled and deleted the display adapter software. I checked for updates, which installed the Intel(R) HD Graphics 520 driver, but the flickering persists on standard user accounts. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My laptop's physical mouse pad is currently physically stuck. I am using a Bluetooth mouse instead, which works fine.
accepted_answer: Try enabling the built-in Administrator Account in Windows and see if that will work. Here are the steps:  1. Right-click on the Start button and choose Command Prompt (Admin) 2.Type the following command: net user administrator /active:yes 3. You should see a message that the command completed successfully. 4. Log out, and you'll see the Administrator account as a choice.   Kindly update us with the result.

title: BSOD loop on new hardware (inc. BSOD on USB insertion)
original_question: Computer Specs (PSU, GPU, CPU, RAM, Motherboard) Windows 10 Pro 64-bit Intel Core i7 7700K @ 4.20GHz Kaby Lake 14nm Technology RAM 16.0GB DDR4 MSI Z270 GAMING PRO CARBON (MS-7A63) (U3E1) ASUS MG279Q (2560x1440@64Hz) ATI Radeon RX 580 Series (MSI) 465GB Samsung SSD 840 EVO 500GB (SSD)32 °C Realtek High Definition Audio Silverstone Strider Gold S Series 80 Gold 850W PSU Speccy Link http://speccy.piriform.com/results/hoGRDXBkFHvy1CFiUJ1807Q http://speccy.piriform.com/results/U2tl25T2LXfHXvxFMhRZCoZ (From Safe Mode, in case it's helpful) Description of problem Hey guys, I've got a brand new system and all of a sudden it's become unusable due to being stuck in a BSOD loop rotating between around 6 different errors. BSOD error notes I've made: Driver overran stack buffer Kmode exception not handled Recource not owned Kernal security check failure Reference by pointer FLTMGR.sys CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED Bad pool caller NTFS.sys Page fault in nonpaged area DPC watchdog violation System thread exception not handled Safe mode is the only way to be able to use my system. The PC can't even remain stable for long enough to reinstall windows! Hardware-wise nothing  has been tweaked beyond how it came in the box. Here are my specs, in case it helps.Here is my report from System Information and here are my mini dumps . When this issue began Two days ago. These may or may not be related, but when playing PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds (PUBG) I got a system lock up accompanied by a plain screen of flat colour (which may actually be close to my theme's colour: dark red and an audio stutter/buzz). Hard power-down is required. As my best guess, this was the when the BSODs started. Recurring issue Yes. If I can get to Safe Mode is it fine though. I can't recall a BSOD in Safe Mode. Cause/Steps to recreate the issue Turn on PC and it'll happen almost instantly. Some BSODs happen before you even get into Windows. Crashes even happen during Windows install process. The only way to reinstall was to take the SSD out and install Windows using another PC before putting the SSD back into my box. Plugging something into USB will almost certainly BSOD it too. What I've tried so far to resolve the issue Win10's inbuilt memory test MemTest86 (8 passes) Tested RAM sticks individually chkdsk + scandisk Rebuild MBR System Restore (BSOD interrupted this) System Reset (can't do it sometimes, other times it BSODs) Startup Repair (BSOD during) Removed GPU and tried onboard GFX Removed all other components and tested successfully separately Updating all motherboard drivers and flashing BIOS Tested one other set of RAM on motherboard compatibility list and still got BSODs Ubuntu seemed to run fine off USB3 There may be more, sorry I've tried so much I've probably forgotten some. Everything seems to point to the motherboard being faulty but I've just got this gut feeling that because safe mode is stable it's not faulty. Before I send it off for RMA and potentially incur a cost for them not finding it faulty I thought I'd try one last time to find an expert who might be able to shine some light on this for me. Thank you in advance for any help you can give me.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My brand new PC is completely unusable because it is stuck in a continuous Blue Screen of Death (BSOD) loop. The system cannot even remain stable long enough to reinstall Windows normally. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * OS: Windows 10 Pro 64-bit * CPU: Intel Core i7 7700K @ 4.20GHz Kaby Lake 14nm Technology * RAM: 16.0GB DDR4 * Motherboard: MSI Z270 GAMING PRO CARBON (MS-7A63) (U3E1) * GPU: ATI Radeon RX 580 Series (MSI) * Storage: 465GB Samsung SSD 840 EVO and a secondary 500GB SSD * Monitor: ASUS MG279Q (2560x1440@64Hz) * Audio: Realtek High Definition Audio * PSU: Silverstone Strider Gold S Series 80 Gold 850W * Logs available: Speccy links (normal and Safe Mode), System Information report, and mini dumps. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The issue started two days ago. * The first crash happened while playing PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds (PUBG). The system locked up with a flat dark red screen and an audio stutter/buzz, requiring a hard power-down. * The BSODs now happen almost instantly when turning on the PC, sometimes before Windows even loads. * Plugging anything into a USB port almost certainly triggers a BSOD. * Crashes occur even during the Windows installation process. * The BSOD loop rotates between several specific errors: - Driver overran stack buffer - Kmode exception not handled - Resource not owned - Kernel security check failure - Reference by pointer - FLTMGR.sys - CRITICAL_PROCESS_DIED - Bad pool caller - NTFS.sys - Page fault in nonpaged area - DPC watchdog violation - System thread exception not handled WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Ran Windows 10's inbuilt memory test and MemTest86 (8 passes). * Tested my RAM sticks individually. * Tested a completely different set of RAM from the motherboard's compatibility list (still resulted in BSODs). * Ran chkdsk and scandisk. * Attempted to rebuild the MBR. * Attempted System Restore, System Reset, and Startup Repair (all failed or were interrupted by BSODs). * Removed the GPU and tried using the onboard graphics. * Removed all other components and tested them successfully on their own. * Updated all MSI motherboard drivers and flashed the BIOS. * Contacted MSI support, but they only suggested basic steps that did not resolve the issue. * Bypassed the installation crash by taking the SSD out, installing Windows onto it using another PC, and putting it back into my machine. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * Hardware-wise, absolutely nothing has been tweaked or overclocked beyond how it came in the box. * Safe Mode is completely stable; I have not experienced a single BSOD while in Safe Mode. * Ubuntu runs perfectly fine off a USB 3.0 drive. * I suspect the motherboard might be faulty, but the fact that Safe Mode is perfectly stable makes me second-guess this.
accepted_answer: Hi,  Yes it definitely sounds like a hardware problem.  Since the computer is new and still under warranty please contact ASUStek Support and the seller. As OEM ASUStek is responsible for the proper operation of both the hardware and Windows. So regardless of the cause it is theirs to resolve. And the unit is under full warranty and may be in need of repairs or replacement. Added that even if you are able to resolve the issue you would want it fully documented just in case there are related problems down the road. accepted_answer: Hello Natalie,  Thank you for responding!  If the first troubleshooting steps did not work, please try these: 1. Use a different Internet connection. 2. Make sure you're using the latest version of Internet Explorer (IE 8). 3. If you have the latest version, reset it. 4. Restart your computer. 5. Re-launch Skype and make a test call. 6. Make sure you're signed in to one device only. Signing in to multiple devices at the same time may affect the call quality.  Let me know if the issue persist.

title: Morph Transition
original_question: I have Office 365 and have the Morph Transition tool in Powerpoint.  The Morph tool seems to only work seamlessly when I use the resize handles on a shape.  But if I change the shape using edit points, Morph does not work.  What happens is the first shape fades out completely and then when it comes to the next slide and the new shape "appears".  So it doesn't "Morph" so to speak.  I have read that you should not change the amount of points, and I haven't.  I've only changed the shape by editing points that were already there.  I've gone through step-by-step instructions on the Microsoft website, and I still can't get this to morph properly.  Oh, and I've also Updated Office with system restart, plus tried repairing Office with a system restart and it didn't fix the problem.  Anyone have any ideas? Is this just me? or is anyone else having this issue?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM The Morph Transition tool in PowerPoint is not working when I modify a shape using edit points. It only works correctly if I alter the shape using the standard resize handles. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Microsoft Office 365 Application: PowerPoint SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I change a shape using edit points and apply the Morph transition, the first shape fades out completely and the new shape simply "appears" on the next slide. It does not actually morph. This happens even though I am strictly editing existing points and have not changed the total number of points on the shape. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Verified that I am not adding or removing edit points, only moving the existing ones. - Followed the step-by-step Morph instructions on the Microsoft website. - Updated Office and performed a system restart. - Ran a repair on Office and performed a system restart. accepted_answer: On the first slide edit points and change the shape by the tiniest amount you can.  Duplicate the slide and edit the p[points to the final shape needed then morph.

title: Unable to connect to Microsoft
original_question: Error message when signing in to Skype on IPad: Unable to connect to Microsoft, check your network settings.Skype version 6.35.54. After I reinstalled the latest version, it worked only once and afterward, I get this error message every time I try to sign in to Skype.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am unable to sign in to Skype on my iPad and am completely blocked by a connection error. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: iPad (Model MC707LL/A; I am not sure if this is an iPad 2) - OS: iOS 9.3.5 - App: Skype for iOS, version 6.35.54 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - Exact error message: "Unable to connect to Microsoft, check your network settings." - The error occurs every time I try to sign in. - When attempting to sign in, it takes a very long time for the Skype site to respond to my iPad. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I reinstalled the latest version of the Skype app. After doing this, it worked exactly once, but the error returned on all subsequent sign-in attempts. - I looked into reverting to an older version (I read somewhere that the iPad version is 6.35.1), but I do not think I have a way to get the older version back. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I rely on Skype to communicate with my siblings abroad.
accepted_answer: Hello TylerFogg,     We want to create the best possible app experience for our Skype users which means we must make some changes along the way. These decisions are hard to make but will enable us to focus our efforts on transitioning Skype from a peer-to-peer video and chat app to a modern, mobile friendly cloud architecture.    Skype on the cloud means you can access all of your chats and shared images on any device with the same Skype account and consume less battery power.   With these, we can no longer get back to the older version of Skype for iPad 6.35.1.   Alternatively, you can uninstall and reinstall Skype for iPad.   To uninstall Skype for iPad, simply touch and hold the Skype icon on your home screen until the icon begins to shake and an “x” appears in the corner of the Skype icon. Release the icon and then tap the “x”. This will delete Skype from iPad. Reboot your device. Reinstall Skype from the App Store and do another reboot before signing back in to Skype.   Hope this helps. Let me know if you need further assistance.   Thank you!

When attempting to use system restore points, I receive a failure notification and the procedure stops.
