title: Embedded Word Document In Excel Icon Issues
original_question: Hello I've embedded a Word document into an Excel file. Everything works great, however, it looks ugly. My main issues are: When I click on the Word file's icon, the icon shows a bounding box around it. The Word file's icon can currently be resized and repositioned anywhere in the Excel document.  So, my questions are: How do I get rid of the bounding box? Right now the embedded Word file seems to be "hovering" over the Excel file rather than contained within a cell. How do I get the file icon to be the content of the cell? Finally, once the above has been achieved, how do I lock the icons size and position inside the cell? Thanks in advance.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have embedded a Word document into an Excel file, but the file icon "hovers" over the spreadsheet instead of acting as the actual content of a cell. I need the icon to be clickable to open the document, contained entirely within a specific cell, locked in size and position, and free of any bounding boxes. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Word SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS Currently, the embedded Word file icon floats over the Excel sheet and can be resized or repositioned anywhere in the document. When I click on the icon, an ugly bounding box appears around it. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched online for "place Word document in Excel cell" but the solutions provided do not actually achieve this. During troubleshooting, I tried a method that positioned the icon perfectly inside the cell, but it made the icon unclickable, meaning the Word document would no longer open. I tried another method that kept the icon clickable, but the bounding box returned and the icon could once again be moved around the spreadsheet. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My ideal solution requires the icon to be completely static, contained within the cell, clickable, and have no bounding box. If making it completely static is impossible, having its movement strictly contained to the cell boundaries would be an acceptable alternative.
accepted_answer: The direct answers to your initial 3 questions are:  A couple of points:  First & foremost: Objects cannot be inserted into cells... period. What Jessica attempted to describe as well as any other 'tutorials' you find may be well-intended but are a misrepresentation of what actually is being done. Essentially, the object can be locked into position on a sheet so that it appears to be contained within a cell. In truth, the object still floats on the surface of the sheet.  Second: Keeping the object anchored in place requires 2 conditions:  As you've found, however, with Protection enabled the object icon is rendered as a static graphic & cannot be opened.  Although it isn't exactly what you want, the only option I can suggest is to insert a Hyperlink to the document in a cell... An icon could be included as well with the Hyperlink in an adjacent cell. The Hyperlink will work even when Protection is enabled to preserve the positioning of the icon. A down side of this method, though, is that a copy of the document is not embedded in the Excel file, so a copy of the document must travel with the workbook.

Opening attachments from websites defaulted to the new Microsoft Edge, and my default for Paint was also changed. I had to manually go into my settings and change these defaults back. - I suspect the afternoon Bing issue might be caused by developers tinkering with the system at that time.
accepted_answer: Searching with Bing does not open a new tab or a new window. I would presume it is the web browser you are using that would be causing a search to open in a new tab/window. Web browser settings are in various different locations in different browsers, but are normally easy to find. You should probably be able to mess with some of the settings and fix your new tab/new window issue.

title: Bing news keeps minimizing (crashing) on surface pro 2

Funny, the Live Tile  updates. I have a Surface Pro2 512 with the newer 4300 processor. I am on Windows 8.1 and I have no pending updates. I have not installed any new programs. I can access the Microsoft Store without problem and all other Metro apps run fine. Bing News seems to have started this behavior on its own. I hope Microsoft can help.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM Whenever I try to open the Bing News app from the modern interface, it briefly looks like it is updating and then immediately minimizes (crashes) to the desktop taskbar. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: Surface Pro 2 (512GB model) with the newer 4300 processor - OS: Windows 8.1 - App: Bing News (Modern/Metro app) - User Profiles: Two accounts configured on the device (one Microsoft account login, one Local account login) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The issue started on Thursday, April 24, 2014. - When the app minimizes to the taskbar, I can click on it and it will try to run, but it minimizes again after a few seconds. - If I open Task Manager, Bing News does not display as a running program at all, even when it is minimized to the taskbar. - The Bing News Live Tile still updates normally. - The crash only occurs when I am logged in via my Microsoft account. If I log in using my Local account, Bing News works perfectly. - All other Metro apps run fine, and I can access the Microsoft Store without any problems. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Uninstalled Bing News, performed a cold boot, and reinstalled the app from the Microsoft Store. - Started the device from a full power-down shutdown and tried launching Bing News first. - Checked for system updates (there are no pending updates, only recent Windows Defender updates that cannot be uninstalled). accepted_answer: Hi there, Welcome to Microsoft Community. Could you check in Event Viewer if there are any errors? To get to Event Viewer: Event Viewer collects all errors and crashes and lists them. It may have an error about Bin News Crashing. Start > Control Panel Administrive Tools Event Viewer Expand Windows Logos and click Application. Are there any erros there? If there is, please list them on here. Uninstall Bing News App and Re-install It. To resolve these issues, install and run the following tool to quickly uninstall all affected apps from your device. After this process is complete, you can reinstall the uninstalled Bing apps from the Windows Store. UninstallAllBingApps.cab Choose Open when you are prompted to open or save UninstallAllBingApps.cab. When the file is opened, double-click UninstallAllBingApps.ps1, select a folder to copy the script to, and then click Extract. From the folder in which you saved the script, find UninstallAllBingApps.ps1, right-click it and select Run with Powershell. If you are prompted to change the execution policy, press Enter to select Yes. Let me know if you still need help after using the above suggestions! Cheers, Leo

accepted_answer: Probably doing nothing wrong.  Did my example search in system32 work or did it fail?  I can't recreate the problem and I don't like to offer any idea that begin with the word "try" but now that you have an example up some other folks might have some ideas.  This has worked for some folks:  Navigate to this folder:  C:UsersYour user nameAppDataLocalMicrosoftWindows1033  Rename the file StructuredQuerySchema.bin to something you can remember like:  StructuredQuerySchema.xxx  Reboot your system (may not be necessary) and check Search again. title: 3D Tools in PowerPoint Not Appearing
original_question: Hello, I have Office 365 subscription and I just installed the latest version of Windows (Windows 10 Creator) where the 3D Tools in PowerPoint is supposed to show in the Insert Menu, but it does not. Any idea what I need to do to get this new feature. I am really looking forward to using it. Appreciate your help! Thanks.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I cannot find the 3D Tools feature in PowerPoint; it is supposed to be located in the Insert Menu, but it is completely missing. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Device: Laptop - OS: Windows 10 Creator (latest version) - Software: PowerPoint (Office 365 subscription) - Other Software: Paint 3D is installed on the system SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The 3D Tools option does not appear in the Insert Menu. - Other new Office features are present and working normally (e.g., the Morph feature is visible in the transition tab). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Opted into the Office Insider program. - Forced a system/software update 2-3 times. - Restarted my computer. - Took a screenshot of my File > Account screen to show my current account and version details. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I am aware the feature rollout was in phases, but recent tweets from Microsoft and others suggest it has gone mainstream (https://twitter.com/microsoftgulf/status/907906826330624000).
accepted_answer: As Debra's alluded to, whether you get the latest features will depend on which version of Office 365 you have.  Do you already have Morph as one of the options on the Transitions tab?  If not, then almost certainly you don't have a version of Office that receives feature updates.  If you do have Morph, then we're off to a good start.  Next step:  Go to File | Account and opt in to the Insider program, which allows you to receive new features early on, before they're more generally released; I'm fairly sure that 3D options are not yet generally out there.  Then force PowerPoint to update.  You may need to do this a time or three, quitting and restarting it in between.  That's what it took to get 3D and some of the other newer features to appear here.

I need to be able to add, change, and sort data on either sheet while maintaining row data integrity. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel 2010 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS I have a first sheet called "Participants" with the columns: Name, Agency, Topic, Comments, Priority, and Contact #. I have a second sheet called "Calendar" with the columns: Name, Agency, Topic, Contacted, Status, and Dates Available. I am able to successfully link the first three columns between the two sheets. However, if I add data to the remaining unlinked columns on the second sheet and then sort the first sheet, the linked data shifts but the manually entered data on the second sheet does not, causing the rows to mismatch. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried working with the VLookup function to manage the data. I also used the conditional formatting highlight cell rule to search for duplicate entries in my data. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT This is a very large workbook, and the name column could potentially have 10,000+ entries. While checking my data, I accidentally found duplicate names; one was an exact match, and another had a trailing space after the name so the program treated it as a different value. I plan to fix these duplicates so all names are unique.
accepted_answer: The value in A2 ("Snoopy") of the Calendar sheet needs to be manually entered.  This can be done using a Data Validation list which refers to column A of the Participants sheet.  Columns B & C in the Calendar sheet need to be formulas like this: In B2: =VLOOKUP($A2,Participants!$A:$C,COLUMN(Participants!$B$1),FALSE) In C2: =VLOOKUP($A2,Participants!$A:$C,COLUMN(Participants!$C$1),FALSE)  That way if the Participants sheet is sorted the Calendar sheet will still look up the correct data.  Hope this helps.  Emily

title: Text isn't properly minimizing under headers in Word 2016
original_question: When I'm going through a large word document inserting headers, any text that is formatted into multiple columns (and all the text below it) does not minimize under the corresponding header. Text that isn't formatted into multiple columns minimizes just fine, but as soon as I format a chunk of text into multiple columns, those columns and any text below them no longer minimize properly (until I get to the next header, at which point the issue is "reset" until I use column formatting agaim).  Here is a photo to illustrate my problem:   I'm running Windows 10 and have an Office 365 home subscription. Is this a feature that's supposed to be there? Or is this an issue I can fix? I want to be able to minimize text under headers to make working with large documents easier, but this issue breaks that functionality.  Thanks for any help.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I am working in a large Word document, any text that is formatted into multiple columns (and all the text below it) fails to minimize under its corresponding header. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Operating System: Windows 10 - Software: Microsoft Word 2016 - Subscription: Office 365 Home SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - Text that is not formatted into multiple columns minimizes perfectly fine. - As soon as I format a chunk of text into multiple columns, those columns and any text below them no longer minimize properly. - The issue "resets" when I get to the next header, at which point minimizing works normally again until I use column formatting. - I suspect the issue is caused by the transition from normal text to columns registering as a section break. - I have a photo that illustrates the problem. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I tested this exact formatting scenario on my friend's laptop, and they are experiencing the exact same issue. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I rely on minimizing text under headers to make navigating and working with large documents easier, but this issue completely breaks that functionality.
accepted_answer: There are a couple of possibilities, but they'll involve additional fiddling with the text if you need to edit it.  One way is to replace the two-column section (and the section breaks at its beginning and end) with a two-column-one-row table and turn off its borders. You'll then have to manually move text (and any images) from the end of the first column to the second column to balance the column lengths. If you later add or remove anything in the table, you'll have to manually rebalance the columns.  Another way is to use two side-by-side text boxes. Use the Size settings on the Drawing Tools tab to make them the same size. To ensure that the text boxes stay where you want them, click Drawing Tools > Position > More Layout Options and set the first box to "Left relative to Column" and the second box "Right relative to Column", both of them "Top relative to Margin". Click in the left box and click the Create Link button on the Drawing Tools tab, and then click in the right box -- now text will "flow" from the left box to the right box. (Note that the boxes don't automatically resize when the text exceeds their total length -- another manual adjustment. They also don't continue to the next page; you'd have to draw another pair of text boxes and link from box 2 to box 3 and then from box 3 to box 4. There's a maximum of 32 linked boxes.)

title: Highlight the answer in a column
original_question: I have a table of data. It is several hundred rows. I show the min, max and mean of a particular column. Is there a way, in addition to getting the answer, to highlight (different color or background) where it is in the column? It would save time trying to find it. Thanks.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have a data table and I calculate the min, max, and mean of a particular column. I want to automatically highlight (using a different color or background) the exact cells inside the data column that match those calculated answers so they are easy to find. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I attempt to format the answers, the formatting only highlights the separate cells where the Max or Min formulas are located. It does not highlight the actual matching numbers (for example, if the Min is 324) inside the data column itself. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I have already set up the formulas to calculate and show the min, max, and mean of the column. I attempted to use conditional formatting to highlight the results, and I tried referencing the Max and Min cells (which took me a bit of time to reference correctly). ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The data table is several hundred rows long, which is why I need the values highlighted directly in the data column to save time searching for them manually.
accepted_answer: I think Aaron means, in the cells in the column with numbers, add three conditional formatting rules to each cell. You can create the rules in one cell and then copy...paste special...formats to the other cells in the column.   One rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MIN of the column and applies the color; the second rule checks if the cell's value is equal to the cell that contains the MAX of the column and applies that color; the third does the same for the average.  So only two to three cells would be colored in the column of numbers.

accepted_answer: While it is tempting to use the DVDs you already have, you might be doing yourself a disservice. title: Limitations in QuickParts for SharePoint online and Word Online
original_question: Is there an article about the limitations in the document properties for SharePoint. I'm in the progress to use a custom content type to add some document specific properties, but before doing that I would like to know the limitations. From my short experiences there might be limitations in the number of characters in text strings. I have not succeeded to edit the QuickPart text using Word Online, get some complains of some add-in But generally I'm interested in some reference documentation describing the limitations so I don't jump into a implementation not feasible Edit: Updated with a link to where the limitations are presented: https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc262787(v=office.16).aspx#Column
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am planning to use a custom content type to add document-specific properties, but I need to understand the limitations of the interconnection between Office Documents and SharePoint Document Libraries before proceeding. Additionally, I am unable to edit QuickPart text using Word Online. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Word Online SharePoint Online Office Documents SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I attempt to edit QuickPart text using Word Online, it fails and I receive an error complaining about an add-in. Based on my brief testing, there appear to be limitations on the number of characters allowed in text strings, and many features do not work as expected. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I attempted to edit QuickPart text directly in Word Online but did not succeed. I searched for reference documentation and found a TechNet article detailing SharePoint column limitations: https://technet.microsoft.com/en-us/library/cc262787(v=office.16).aspx#Column ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I am researching this to avoid jumping into an implementation that is not feasible. I have experience reporting design faults to Microsoft using a Premier Support agreement (which typically takes 9-12 months to result in a change), and I am trying to determine if these specific limitations have already been reported as faults through that channel.
accepted_answer: Helpful link, thanks.  Unfortunately I cannot give you the whole picture, or even anything like the whole picture, but some things to look out for:  a. the Sharepoint data types do not map all that well onto the Word content control types. (There are a lot more of the former, for one thing).  b. Some of the mapping, in particular the presentation choices, used to be better when the old Document Information Panel (DIP) was available, in the sense that the display *in the DIP* conformed better to the data type in Sharepoint. But I do not think for example you can present a multiple choice as a set of radio buttons in Word.  c. As far as I know, the current version of Word Online lets you see content controls (and even the content control Name tags) but does not let you edit their contents. I think you have to use an Office (Javascript) Add-in to do that. However, even that may depend on whether you are using the "public" version of Word Online or one hosted by a private SharePoint site, although I assume that the latter version always catches up to the former .  d. When you edit in Desktop Word, what Word actually looks at to try to validate the data in a content control is the XML schema that is generated by SharePoint and inserted along with the Server metadata. But the user does not necessarily get to see that they have invalid data until they click outside the control or try to save. But some of the constraints that you might think would be applied are not - for example, if you specify a multiline type in SharePoint, you can specify a maximum line count, and AFAIK you do not get to specify a character count limit. But when you enter a new line in a plain text content control in Word, what Word inserts is a hard line return, and the resulting lines do not appear to count as lines as far as SharePoint is concerned. Further, Word seems to impose a 255 character maximum whatever SharePoint allows.  e. Because the Word checkbox content control type was not one of the original types, SharePoint does not use it. (Although I think perhaps it should, now). So the obvious user interface element for Yes/No type values is not used. Instead, you get a dropdown that presents "true" and "false" choices, and theya re literally those choices because that's what gets sent in the XML.  f. As a general rule, indexed types are probably not going to work as you might like. e.g. a Lookup just presents a number, not the text actually referenced by the lookup. And I don't think stuff such as Person/Group or Managed MetaData types will work properly. **EDIT: At least some of the stuff that you used to be able to do in the DIP you can now do in BackStage in the Property editing area. In fact some of it is better in thte sense that you do get to see lookups etc. from SharePoint.

title: Formula stopped working
original_question: I have a spreadsheet that I have been using for ten months, I simply place the data in the correct columns, and the COUNTIF formulas calculate how many people of a certain profession and pay grade exists. This has worked for ten months, today I go to execute the same thing I have always done and it will not work. Interestingly enough, it work for the first 80% of the data in the worksheet...my mind is blown!    I have attempted to repaste in to the various spreadsheets I've saved over the last ten months, I've tried erasing all of the data and re-inputting, I've checked to make sure the Automatic calculation is on, I have ensured I don't have a circular formula, and I've tried changing the formatting from General to Text...nothing has worked.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I have an Excel spreadsheet I have been using for ten months where I paste data into columns, and COUNTIFS formulas calculate how many people of a certain profession and pay grade exist. Today, the formulas suddenly stopped calculating my new data correctly. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The exact formula I am using is: `=COUNTIFS(Battalion!$D:$D,"CPT",Battalion!$I:$I,"25A")` Strangely, the formula successfully calculates for the first 80% of the data in the worksheet, but does not work for the remaining 20%. I am unable to share the actual workbook or post a sample file because it contains confidential information. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - Repasted the data into various backup spreadsheets I have saved over the last ten months. - Erased all of the data and re-inputted it. - Verified that "Automatic calculation" is turned on. - Checked to ensure I do not have any circular formulas. - Changed the cell formatting from General to Text. - Tested with old data: I placed "by name" data from an old spreadsheet into my newest version, and the formulas calculated normally. - Replaced the data in the D column with my new data (while keeping the old data in other columns), and the formula continued to work. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT Through my testing with old and new data, I have narrowed the problem down specifically to the data in the I column.
accepted_answer: Check the data for invisible leading or trailing blanks.  You could use the Len() function to compare what you see with what the cell contains. Or you could use   =SEARCH(" ",A1)  and copy down. If it returns a number, there is a blank in the cell. Also, if the data is copied from a web site, it may contain the non-breaking space, which is different from a regular space. To check for these, use  =SEARCH(CHAR(160),A1)

title: There is no login box on windows 8.1 when I try to log in
original_question: I turned on my laptop and the usual screen with the date and time showed up. I clicked to open up the login screen. The password box was not there but my name and email and my picture was there (which, by the way is the default one). I tried turning off then turning my laptop back on and it didn't work. I tried changing my password the log in box was still not there. I tried changing my name on Microsoft live and it did not update my name on my laptop. please help this is really urgent as I need to do work on my laptop but cannot log in
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I cannot log into my laptop because the password entry box is completely missing from the Windows login screen. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 Device: Laptop Account Type: Microsoft Live account SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I turn on my computer, the usual screen with the date and time appears. When I click to open the login screen, my name, email, and default profile picture are visible, but there is no box to type my password. There is no power icon visible on the screen. Nothing happens when I click on my profile picture. This issue started right after my computer installed recent Microsoft updates. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried turning the laptop off and back on several times, but the result is exactly the same. I tried changing my password, but the login box still did not appear. I tried changing my name on Microsoft Live, but the new name did not update on my laptop screen. I tried clicking my profile picture to see if it would prompt the password box, but nothing happened. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I have a screenshot of my lock screen available (with my surname and email blocked out for safety). This is extremely urgent as I need to access my laptop to do my work.
accepted_answer: Is there perhaps a left-pointing arrow near your account tile on the login screen?  You can try that to access alternate accounts.  If that doesn't work, let's try to restore back to the last day it was working.  At the bottom of the login screen, you'll see a power icon.  Hold the shift key on your keyboard while you choose the Restart option.  After a few seconds, some options will appear.  Here you can use troubleshooting and then System Restore.  This restores backups of your registry and system settings, but doesn’t touch any of your files. It only takes a couple of minutes.  There are also more intense recovery options (refresh and reset), but I do not recommend those right now, because they are more advanced.

title: I deleted Temp Folder and StartUp Menu on accident now some apps are missing...
original_question: Ok,so here is the problem, which honestly I've had for months now...I was trying to cleanup some files on my computer,well i read somewhere that you don't need your Temp folder so i deleted it i also read something about StartMenu folder taking up a lot of space......so i deleted it, it ask for Administrator permission so I clicked ok and it deleted the files, not only that but i deleted it from my recycle bin. really important things such as Desktop and Shop were gone! I was able to get Desktop Back from another forum post that had a download which currently now is fine, but that not the worst part, my Change PC setting's is gone as well,when i click it nothing happens and i mean nothing if i also try changing my account picture on the metro screen as well nothing happens just....nothing. If anyone has a solution please post it, also another thing that has been happening rapidly. Every time i turn off my PC the next day i turn it on my WiFi is disconnected,if it's not then in the middle of the day it will say *WIFI NAME* Limited and i get disconnected, what does it mean and how do i fix it, I've run Virus scans with Norton and nothing has come up well something but i fixed it,anyways PLEASE HELP!!!
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I accidentally deleted my Temp and StartMenu folders a few months ago while trying to free up space, which caused several apps and system settings to disappear or stop working. I am also experiencing a separate issue where my WiFi frequently disconnects. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 Antivirus: Norton SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The problems started a few months ago after I deleted the folders. Important items like "Desktop" and "Shop" completely disappeared. "Change PC settings" is gone/unresponsive; when I click it, absolutely nothing happens. When I try to change my account picture on the Metro screen, nothing happens. My WiFi is disconnected every time I turn on my PC the next day. In the middle of the day, my network will suddenly say "[WIFI NAME] Limited" and I get disconnected. Some files were restored, but lots of things are still missing. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I used a download from another forum post which successfully brought my Desktop back. I ran a virus scan with Norton; it found something, but I fixed it. I checked my System Restore points, but the only available ones are from a few days ago, which is long after the problem started. I checked my Recycle Bin to restore the files, but I had already emptied it. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I deleted the folders because I read online that the Temp folder wasn't needed and the StartMenu folder took up a lot of space. When deleting the StartMenu folder, it asked for Administrator permission, which I clicked "OK" to grant.
accepted_answer: Some files can be deleted from the Temp folder, but not the folder itself because Windows uses it to hold temporary files in.  It's a bit like your car and it's gas tank. You can drain it, but you still have a fully functional automobile. But if you remove the gas tank, then you're not going to be going anywhere until you replace it.  You now find yourself in that situation with your computer. Until you replace the Temp folder and its contents, your machine is not going to function properly.  I think you're looking at having to Refresh the system in order to get it going again. What you'll have to do first is to create a Recovery disk and copy the manufacturer's partition to that. This means you'll have to go buy an external hard drive with at least 60GB free. But bearing in mind that the cost of a 60GB hard disk and one with a 500GB capacity is barely a matter of cents, you'll be better off with the large capacity drive which you can use for other purposes later.  To create the Recovery drive, hit Windows key to open the Start screen with the tiles and start typing the word "Recovery". You'll see the option called "Create a recovery drive" appear on the right. Click that and then click Next. Here's a screenshot.      Then follow instructions in this article: http://windows.microsoft.com/en-gb/windows-8/restore-refresh-reset-pc

title: Window 7 ISO
original_question: I would like to reinstall Windows 7 on my computer. I have no disk but have the key. However, I am unable to connect to the internet with the computer due to some kind of technical hitch/corruption. I have tried to download the Windows 7 ISO on a different computer but  I only receive the error message: " We encountered a problem with the validation request". Can I only download the ISO to the computer on which it is installed or is there some other reason for this? If the former is true what alternatives do I have? Thank you in advance for any advice and suggestions.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need to reinstall Windows 7 on my computer, but I do not have the installation disk and the computer is currently unable to connect to the internet. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Operating System: Windows 7 - I have the Windows 7 product key, but no physical installation media. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The computer needing the reinstall cannot connect to the internet due to some kind of technical hitch or corruption. - When I tried downloading the ISO on a different computer, I received the exact error message: "We encountered a problem with the validation request". - During my attempts, I encountered a message or restriction that appeared to be a 24-hour embargo on downloading the file again. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I attempted to download the Windows 7 ISO using a second, working computer. - I spent over 30 minutes downloading the ISO file. - When I prepared to create the installation disk, I thought the file had mysteriously disappeared. I later realized my mistake: I had successfully downloaded it, but saved it in a different directory than I intended. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - I am unsure if the initial validation error happened because the ISO can only be downloaded directly onto the computer where it is registered/installed.
accepted_answer: If your system came with Windows pre-installed from the factory so that makes it an OEM installation.  Microsoft does not allow you do download installation ISOs for OEM licenses (unless it is a System Builder).  Visit here:  https://heidoc.net/joomla/technology-science/microsoft/67-microsoft-windows-and-office-iso-download-tool  Download the Windows ISO Downloader to your desktop, run/launch it (nothing gets installed on your computer) and a few clicks will get you to whatever Windows Edition (Home, Pro, etc.), architecture (bitness) and language you desire.  Be sure you download the same Windows Edition (Pro, Home, etc.) that matches your original installation.  For example:     Download away.  You can use the Windows USB/DVD Creation tool to burn the ISO you downloaded to create a bootable CD/DVD or USB:  https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/windows-usb-dvd-download-tool  Or you can use free/popular third party programs like:  Rufus  (to create a bootable USB thumb drive in just a few clicks)  or  ImgBurn  (to create a bootable CD/DVD)  If you install ImgBurn be sure to choose a custom installation and uncheck these boxes during the installation:

title: Linking cells in a table across multiple worksheets
original_question: So I have an excel document that has 100+ worksheets in it. We use this to list all of the various materials that we need to purchase for a project. For example, if we are using 5 different sizes of screws, we would list all of these out and then have a total quantity needed cell beside it.  Most of these sheets are identically set up in the same manner, with the exception that the name of the sheet is different and the various quantities will vary on each sheet. On our main page (Master BOM), we type out all of the different items and then we go onto the various worksheets (UNIT NAMES) that use those products. On these pages we type the quantity we need and we have it set up to where all of these sheet quantities are totaled on the Master BOM sheet. What I am now trying to do is create another sheet that lists the names of all of the worksheets and then beside these names list the total number of units being made. For example, if we are building 3 different sized boxes named Box A, Box B, and Box C, and we are ordering 2,4, & 6, respectively, I have a cell labeled Box A and the cell beside it has a quantity of 2 in it. Make sense? So I have my list completely done and the quantities listed beside them. What I am needing to do is have that corresponding quantity automatically link to the worksheet for that particular unit. So using my example above, I have Box A listed with 2 quantities. I want that 2 (or whatever number I have to update it to) to automatically link to the worksheet that I have for Box A (and yes the worksheet is labeled as Box A already). Is there a way to do this without me having to go through every single sheet and type that it is equal to a certain cell? I do have Kutools if that helps.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need a way to automatically link unit quantities from a summary sheet to 100+ individual worksheets based on matching sheet names, without having to manually type the cell references into every single sheet. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel Kutools add-in is installed SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS I have an Excel document with over 100 identically formatted worksheets used for project materials. The individual worksheets are named after specific units (e.g., "Box A", "WPE79", "WPE106"). I have a "Master BOM" sheet where we list all items, which totals the quantities pulled from the individual unit worksheets. I created a new sheet called "Takeoff" that lists the names of all the individual worksheets alongside the total number of units being made for each (e.g., Box A has a quantity of 2). I need the quantity number on the "Takeoff" sheet to automatically link to the specific worksheet with that matching name. If a quantity changes on the "Takeoff" sheet, it must automatically update on the corresponding individual sheet. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I have completely finished building the "Takeoff" list with the unit names and their corresponding quantities. I have already labeled all the individual worksheets with their respective unit names. I uploaded my original file to Dropbox: https://www.dropbox.com/s/xy9509ylddxk7rp/bom%202055.xlsx?dl=0 I also created and uploaded a simplified version of the file to Dropbox: https://www.dropbox.com/s/835eauljjhg7em4/Sample%20BOM.xlsx?dl=0 ADDITIONAL CONTEXT My workbook utilizes Tables with structured formulas (for example: =Table1101113[@[Unit of Measure]]). The worksheets currently contain merged cells (specifically columns D and E in rows 2, 3, and 4).
accepted_answer: That's great The snip shows the formula to use Note depending on what is in Takeoff you will need to adjust the range: =VLOOKUP(D2,Takeoff!B4:C20,2,FALSE)  With lots of worksheets it would be a chore do enter a formula in everyone. The trick is to group the worksheets first: 1) click on the BoxA tab 2)Hold down SHIFT and click on thtab of the last worksheet needing the formula; the tabs will now all be bright. 3)Type the formula; each worksheet will get a copy 4) Right click the tab of BoxA and select Ungroup   Hope this gets you on the right road

title: Placing a Check Mark in a cell if another cells number fall with in an aproved range
original_question: How do I formulate cell Q23 if J23 fall with in the approved rang pre-established in G23? for example G23 = 98-100 user types in any number within the approved rang in J23, Q23 should auto populate with a check mark. and if the user types any number outside the approved rang in J23, Q23 will need to auto populate a red X.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need cell Q23 to auto-populate with a check mark if the number typed into J23 falls within an approved range established in cell G23, and a red X if it falls outside that range. My problem is that Q23 does not know what range to use because G23 is not a set value, but rather a formula. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Microsoft Excel SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS My formula for Q23 cannot determine the correct range to use out of G23. I also do not know where or how to access the "edit formatting rule" menu. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tested a formula that worked perfectly when G23 contained a static value, but it does not work with my actual setup where G23 contains a formula. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The formula generating the range in G23 is: `=IF(G19="Asphalt Stabilized Base","84-98",IF(G19="C_Coarse_Surface","95-100",IF(G19="D_Fine_Surface","0")))`. For example, if G23 evaluates to "98-100", typing any number within that range in J23 should show a check mark in Q23, while typing a number outside that range should show a red X.
accepted_answer: Enter this formula in cell Q23:  =IF(AND(J23>=VALUE(LEFT(G23,FIND("-",G23)-1)),J23<=VALUE(RIGHT(G23,FIND("-",G23)))),2,1)  And then apply this conditional formatting rule to Q23 (note that this is from Excel 2010, but should still apply. Also ensure you check the Show Icon Only box):

title: Apply updated Notes Master to my slides
original_question: I recall it was difficult to apply it to all slides (unless this has been changed), but now I can't find the option to update a single slide at a time. It used to be a right click on the notes page. Has the command moved?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to apply changes I made in the Notes Master (font, font size, slide position, and notes text box size) to my existing slides, but I cannot find the command to update a single slide at a time. There used to be an "Apply Notes Master" command accessible by right-clicking on the notes page, but it seems to be missing. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * PowerPoint for Office 365 (specifically mentioned Office 365 2016) * Version 15.37 (confirmed via the About box) * No pending updates (running the most recent version available to me) * Also working with Apple Keynote SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When I modify the Notes Master, some slides successfully update to the new format, but others do not. * Slides pasted from various other presentations retain their original formatting and refuse to change when the Notes Master is updated. * Some slides have multiple text boxes stacked on top of one another; the hidden bottom box updates to the new master, but a stray box on top remains stuck in the old format. * On certain slides (likely those originally created in Keynote), I am able to change the font, but the software completely prevents me from applying formatting like bold or italic. * Changes to the Notes Master do not fix the placement or size of the slide image on the notes page if it was previously moved or resized. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I made my changes in the View tab > Notes Master. * I searched everywhere on the notes page for the old right-click command to apply the master. * I successfully updated headers and footers using the Header/Footer window, but this did not resolve the notes text and layout issues. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am working with a massive slide library of 3,500 slides and am currently on a strict deadline. * These presentations have a messy history: they contain many different slide layouts and template changes, feature slides copied and pasted among various presentations, and have been switched back and forth between multiple versions of PowerPoint and Keynote. * I have found a tedious workaround for the text formatting: I cut the notes text from the notes page, delete the notes placeholder, switch to Normal view, and paste "Keep text only" into the notes pane below the slide. This recreates the text box with the current Notes Master formatting. * Because the text workaround does not fix slide placement/size issues, my only workaround for layout issues is to create a brand new slide, copy the slide elements onto it, and then copy the notes over.
accepted_answer: As I noted though a quick test showed it failed on a Mac for me.  IF no-one has altered the default names of placeholders this might work on a Mac (ALWAYS WORK ON A COPY)  Sub DodgyFix()    Dim strText As String    Dim oshp As Shape    Dim osld As Slide    Dim refImage As Shape    For Each oshp In ActivePresentation.NotesMaster.Shapes       If oshp.Name Like "Slide Image*" Then          Set refImage = oshp       End If    Next oshp     ActiveWindow.ViewType = ppViewNotesPage    For Each osld In ActivePresentation.Slides       ActiveWindow.View.GotoSlide (osld.SlideIndex)       For Each oshp In osld.NotesPage.Shapes          If Not refImage Is Nothing Then             If oshp.Name Like "Slide Image*" Then                oshp.Left = refImage.Left                oshp.Top = refImage.Top                oshp.Width = refImage.Width                oshp.Height = refImage.Height             End If          End If          If oshp.Name Like "Notes Placeholder*" Then             If oshp.HasTextFrame Then                If oshp.TextFrame.HasText Then                    oshp.TextFrame.TextRange.Copy                   oshp.Delete                   osld.NotesPage.Shapes(osld.NotesPage.Shapes.Count).Select                   ActiveWindow.View.Paste                End If             End If           End If       Next oshp    Next osld End Sub

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Acer Iconia W701 tablet (also referred to as Iconia 701) * Operating System: Windows 8.1 * Bluetooth Manufacturer: Atheros (spelled "Aethros" in my system) * Headset default PIN: "0000" SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * My Bluetooth keyboard connects to the tablet perfectly fine, but audio devices and phones fail. * When I try to connect the Bluetooth headset, it connects initially but disconnects within a few seconds. * When I try to connect my Android mobile phone, it connects after entering a 6-digit PIN, but also disconnects within a few seconds. * When I use "Add Device", it does not diagnose my issue and instead shows unrecognized phone models (possibly devices from outside my location). * When I try to update the Bluetooth driver, I get a message saying "driver is up to date and best driver is installed." * In Device Manager, there is a "Yellow !" warning icon next to the Bluetooth audio device under the sound and hardware section. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I ran the Windows Troubleshooter, but it just redirected me to the Microsoft community website. * I opened services.msc, set the Bluetooth service to "Automatic" mode, and stopped and restarted the service. * I checked Device Manager to restart the device and update the drivers. * I tried using the "Add Device" menu to pair the headset and my phone. accepted_answer: Hi David,  Apologize for the late reply.  You can go to the Acer website and first download the Bluetooth driver for model Acer Iconia W701. http://www.acer.co.in/ac/en/IN/content/drivers  Now, you can reinstall the Bluetooth driver. a. title: MISSING SPACE
original_question: Being a novice at computer technology, and because I only use it to communicate, game and research matters., I'm confused as to where my space is being fed, I have an Atom notebook/laptop by Asus, other than the files necessary to operate the system, I have nothing else but 1 game file loaded on it. it has not completed any of its new updates they are all partials fighting each other when space becomes available., I have erased all the free apps that came along with it save for the 4 needed ones, desk top, internet, etc... there is nothing left to erase. Oh and it crashed my MICROSOFT VISUAL STUDIO 15 and 13 which cannot be cleaned for lack of space.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My hard drive is completely out of space and sits at zero bytes available, which is causing my programs to crash, preventing system updates from completing, and completely blocking me from using my computer. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * **OS:** Windows RT 8.1 * **Device:** ASUS ATOM notebook/laptop, Model # X205TA * **Processor:** Intel(R) ATOM(TM), 1329Mhz, 4 core(s), 4 Logical Pro * **Memory:** Installed Physical Memory (RAM) = 2.00 GB, Total Physical Memory = 847 MB, Total Virtual Memory = 2.28 GB, Available Virtual Memory = 880 MB, Page File Space = 351 MB * **External Hardware:** WD Passport Ultra (500 GB removable storage), ASUS External Slim DVD-RW Removable device * **Cloud Storage:** 1.02 Terabytes of OneDrive online storage, Dropbox, and an additional 500 MB of cloud space * **Installed Software:** Windows, Passport, Microsoft downloads, Web Storage, Asus programs, Office 365 Home Edition, Microsoft Visual Studio 2010, 2013, and 2015 (and their components), plus 1 game file SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * My hard drive is at zero space, and my notebook is threatening to crash. * Windows updates cannot complete; they are just partial downloads fighting each other for space. * Microsoft Visual Studio 13 and 15 crashed and cannot be cleaned due to the lack of space. * Dropbox refuses to sync or add files because the local hard drive is at zero. * Files are missing components because of the lack of space, making them incomplete. * I receive exact pop-up error messages stating verbatim: "cannot continue not enough disc space" or "missing [file name] because of one of two reasons... either not enough disc space or file corruption". WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I erased all the free apps that came with the laptop except for four necessary ones (desktop, internet, etc.). * I erased even more programs (some of which I am finding are important) just to free up enough space to reply to support. * I had my sister go through my files to check for duplicate files, but she found nothing and was baffled. * I linked my Dropbox to try and offload files, but it failed due to the local drive having zero space. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am a novice at computer technology and only use this device to communicate, game, and research. * I have been told I must keep 2GB of local storage open in order to utilize my 1.02 TB and 500 MB of cloud space. * My 500 GB WD Passport Ultra is currently full and being used to hold my "history". * I am trying to figure out if I can use my ASUS DVD-RW to burn my history to a DVD, wipe the WD Passport clean to use as temporary storage, and free up enough local hard drive space to finally access and utilize my Terabyte of cloud storage.
accepted_answer: Hi,   Thank you for posting your query in Microsoft Community.   I can imagine the inconvenience you are experiencing and will certainly help you in the right direction to fix the issue. However, I would appreciate if you could answer a few questions to narrow down the issue in order to provide you with better assistance.   As per the issue description the space of your HDD get used up and no free space remains there even though you have limited apps installed. So, it seems that the issue is related to the HDD. I would suggest you to try the steps provided below and see if it helps you to fix the issue.    Method 1:  Step 1: Run Disk Clean Up  First, I suggest you to run Windows Disk Cleanup tool to clean your hard disk from all unnecessary files that might be causing this issue on your computer.  You may refer the steps in the article below to run disk clean up: http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/delete-files-using-disk-cleanup#delete-files-using-disk-cleanup=windows-8  Step 2: Perform disk check  After successfully running Disk Cleanup tool, also run Check Disk to check the hard disk for errors if any and automatically repair them.    NOTE: The root drive has to be C: and the command may schedule a task at the system startup, if it does, then press Y to schedule a task and restart the computer to perform chkdsk.  Important:While performing chkdsk on the hard drive if any bad sectors are found on the hard drive when chkdsk tries to repair that sector if any data available on that might be lost  Method 2:Run Memory Diagnostics  I would also suggest you to run Windows Memory Diagnostics utility. Windows 8.1 includes built-in features to help you identify and diagnose problems with memory. Refer to the steps below: Method 3: Run a Malware Scan  The Microsoft Safety Scanner is a free downloadable security tool that provides on-demand scanning and helps remove viruses, spyware, and other malicious software. It works with your existing antivirus software. Kindly follow the below steps to run the  Microsoft safety scanner. Important:Any data files that are infected may only be cleaned by deleting the file entirely, which means there is a potential for data loss.  Note: The Microsoft Safety Scanner expires 10 days after being downloaded. To rerun a scan with the latest anti-malware definitions, download and run the Microsoft Safety Scanner again.  Hope this will fix the issue, if not let us know with the updated status and we will be happy to assist you further.  Regards,

title: Urgent: What is the fix for PowerPoint for MAC Version 15.32 ERROR: The image part with relationship ID rid2 was not found in the file

Is there a fix? If there is no fix, it means that PowerPoint for MAC is unreliable, therefore unusable.  I need some help here, otherwise this has to be totally rebuilt in MAC PowerPoint 2008 or Keynote.   Is there a fix for this instead of shucking PowerPoint 2016. This represents hundreds of hours and I'm sure I am not the only one with this problem. This is a real problem.  Any suggestions as to a fix, other than >Change Picture? Thanks in advance.

Both my main presentation file and my backup file have become corrupted. title: Skype sign-in on iphone with comma in the username
original_question: I have a comma in my skype user name. So, how do I sign in on my iPhone?  Is there a special key stroke pattern to get a comma? Thanks Ken
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I cannot type my Skype username to sign in on my iPhone because the keyboard presented on the login screen is missing the comma key. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Device: iPhone App: Skype for iOS (latest version, updated a few days ago) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS The comma is completely missing from the regular, numeric, and symbol keypads on the Skype sign-in screen. This issue started a few days ago immediately after a recent Skype update. For the two years prior to this update, I had no problem locating the comma on the login screen. Interestingly, once I am successfully logged into the app, it presents the normal, full keyboard containing the comma and all other characters. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched through the regular, numeric, and symbol keypads. I held down the period key, but it only presented a period and ellipses. I held down several other keys for a few seconds to check for hidden characters, but found no comma. I tried adjusting my iPhone keyboard preferences, but it did not help. I visited the local Microsoft store (Valley Fair mall in San Jose, CA) to ask for help, but the employees and service techs were stumped. I also went to the Apple store to look for an aftermarket external keyboard. accepted_answer: Hello Brian,  Welcome to Skype Community!  It's unusual that you are not seeing a comma on your keyboard when signing in as it is one of the most commonly used characters.  If you have changed your keyboard settings, I suggest that you revert it to the default one.  Alternatively, you may find a comma online or from a text message and copy it. Then, paste it on the log in page of the Skype app.  I hope the information above helps.  Regards,

title: Windows RT Desktop Notification Tray Battery Meter Query for Dual Batteries
original_question: Hi My system is as follows: * Nokia Lumia 2520 (note, this is a tablet, not a mobile phone) * Windows RT I have the above tablet and purchased the keyboard to accompany it; the keyboard incidentally has a battery which complements the main battery in the tablet.  Prior to a recent full system refresh, the battery meter displayed both on the Windows 8 interface, but also in the notification area of the desktop recognised and displayed both batteries and gave a sensible total percentage charge.  For example, when both the tablet and keyboard batteries are quite fully charged, the overall charge is displayed as 100% or slightly less. However, since the full system refresh, the overall charge percentage is making little sense.  For example, at the current time, charge percentages are as follows: * Tablet battery: 100% * Keyboard battery: 1% * Overall charge: 13% I am aware that the tablet battery provides significantly more battery time than the keyboard battery and so am confused as to why the overall charge displayed is so low. Prior to the full system refresh, I am very sure that in the scenario above for the tablet and keyboard battery charges, the overall charge would have more logically been far higher, perhaps at least 80%. I did notice that the overall charge status in terms of 'plugged in/ charging/ not charging' seems to report the status of the keyboard battery, rather than the tablet battery and wonder if the battery meter is somehow inappropriately prioritising the keyboard battery, which is essentially a 'slave' battery. Would anybody therefore know why the above would be occurring and also perhaps how Windows RT tends to handle dual batteries? I can confirm that as of 19/01/16, system update confirms the tablet is fully up to date, so I would assume that any previous fixes that may have been implemented are already installed. Thank you for your time. Best regards Kim
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM After a full system refresh, the battery meter on my tablet is displaying inaccurate overall charge percentages and incorrect charging statuses for my dual-battery setup. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Nokia Lumia 2520 tablet * OS: Windows RT (Windows 8 interface) * Accessory: Accompanying physical keyboard with a built-in secondary battery * System Status: Fully up to date as of 19/01/16 SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The overall charge percentage makes no mathematical sense. For example, with the tablet battery at 100% and the keyboard battery at 1%, the overall charge displays as 13%. (Because the tablet battery has a much larger capacity, this should logically display at around 80%). * The overall charge status ('plugged in/ charging/ not charging') seems to inappropriately report the status of the keyboard battery rather than the main tablet battery. * The battery meter shows the keyboard battery charging extremely slowly, only moving from 6% to 7% after being plugged in for over an hour. * Despite the battery meter stating the keyboard battery is 'plugged in' and 'charging', the AC adapter plugged into the mains is completely cold, indicating no actual power is being drawn. * The main tablet battery continues to charge quickly and fully without issue. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I performed a full system refresh (which is when this issue started). * I ran Windows Update and confirmed the system has all current updates installed as of 19/01/16. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * Prior to the system refresh, the battery meter in both the Windows 8 interface and the desktop notification area correctly recognized both batteries and gave a sensible total percentage. * The keyboard is just over one year old, has not been used excessively, and has only been charged a small number of times, so a physical battery failure is highly unlikely. * Because the AC adapter is cold, I suspect both batteries might actually be fully charged and the battery meter is simply displaying false information.
accepted_answer: Hi,  Thank you for contacting Microsoft Community.  I will certainly help you with this.   I would suggest you to go through the below steps and links:  Step 1: Run the power troubleshooter:  a. Press Windows key+X. b. Click on Control Panel. c. Click on Troubleshooting. d. On the left pane, click on view all. e. From the list, click on power and run the troubleshooter.  Steps 2: Try to run the command in command prompt and check if it helps:  a. Press Windows key+X. b. Click on Command prompt with admin. c. Enter the below command: powercfg -setdcvalueindex SCHEME_CURRENT 19cbb8fa-5279-450e-9fac-8a3d5fedd0c1 12bbebe6-58d6-4636-95bb-3217ef867c1a 3 d. After that enter the command: powercfg -setactive scheme_current  Also refer the links below:  Surface battery and power https://www.microsoft.com/surface/en-us/support/hardware-and-drivers/battery-and-power?os=windows-rt-8.1  Tips to save battery power http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-8/tips-save-battery-power  Battery: Frequently asked questions http://windows.microsoft.com/en-GB/windows-8/battery-meter-frequently-asked-questions  I hope this helps. Please let us know the results. Feel free to write us back for any further assistance with Windows, we’ll be glad to assist you. Did you made any changes to the PC prior to this issue? Have you installed all pending updates on the pc?

title: Toshiba Satellite S50-C keyboard not working properly after Windows 10 Creators Update
original_question: Earlier this year I started noticing that certain keys wouldn't always take. I would be typing and would realize that the shift key or space had not performed its function. But if I pressed deliberately and firmly, it would work. Over time it seemed to get worse, or perhaps I just noticed it more. The right shift key and space tend to be the worst, but 4 on the number row and sometimes numbers on the keypad also do not want to work. The problem is random and sporadic. Sometimes so bad I have difficulty even typing out a short message. Other times I can use the computer for days with minimal issue. The Toshiba troubleshooter and some online sources advise uninstalling the keyboard driver and restarting the computer. I have done this a number of times. Sometimes it seems like it may help for a little while, but the problem always comes back. I'm out of warranty, so they refuse to let me even talk to tech support without charging me. In the list of drivers for my computer, they do not have a keyboard driver. I've seen indications that some people have trouble with conflicts with a Lenovo/Synaptics driver. My touchpad is ELAN, not Synaptics, so that isn't it (although I did try uninstalling and reinstalling the touchpad driver anyway, just in case). I booted Ubuntu from a flash drive and had no problems with the keyboard. Again, with any test it's hard to be sure, given the random and sporadic nature of this issue, but it seems pretty sure that it's a software problems that only developed after the Windows 10 Creator Update. Based on other tips I found online, I have tried removing the battery and waiting for several minutes before starting the computer, and plugging in a USB keyboard, typing and removing the USB keyboard. The problem always comes back. It seems my next course of action may be to factory reset the computer and hope that fixes it--although not only is that a major hassle, but I wonder if, once the computer is restored and updates, the problem will not simply return. Does anyone know where I can find a Satellite S50-C keyboard driver? Toshiba has let me down and I can't seem to find anything. I've found a few Windows 7 drivers for older Toshiba laptops, but I need something explicitly designed to work on Windows 10... ideally something for my model of laptop. Or is there some other solution to this issue?
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My laptop keyboard is not working properly; certain keys randomly fail to register when I am typing unless I press them deliberately and firmly. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Toshiba Satellite S50-C laptop * OS: Windows 10 * Touchpad: ELAN (not Synaptics) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The right shift key and space bar are the most frequently affected. * The '4' key on the number row and sometimes numbers on the keypad also fail to register. * The issue is completely random and sporadic. Sometimes it is so bad I have difficulty typing a short message, while other times I can use the computer for days with minimal issues. * The problem started earlier this year, specifically developing after the Windows 10 Creators Update, and seems to have progressively gotten worse. * When I tried manually downloading and installing a Windows update, I received an error message stating that it was "already installed." WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Uninstalled the keyboard driver and restarted the computer multiple times. This sometimes helps for a little while, but the problem always comes back. * Uninstalled and reinstalled the ELAN touchpad driver just in case there was a conflict. * Booted Ubuntu from a flash drive and tested the keyboard; I experienced absolutely no problems while using Ubuntu. * Removed the laptop battery, waited for several minutes, and restarted the computer. * Plugged in a USB keyboard, typed with it, and then removed it. * Checked Windows Update, which reports that my device is up to date. * Searched the Toshiba website for a replacement keyboard driver. They do not list a keyboard driver for my specific model, and I can only find Windows 7 drivers for older Toshiba laptops. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * My laptop is out of warranty, so Toshiba refuses to let me speak to tech support without charging me. * Because the keyboard works perfectly in Ubuntu, I am highly confident this is a software problem tied to Windows 10. * I read online that some users experience keyboard conflicts with Lenovo/Synaptics drivers, but I have confirmed my touchpad is ELAN. * I am considering doing a factory reset, but it is a major hassle and I am worried the problem will simply return once the computer restores and updates itself.
accepted_answer: As an additional step, you may reach out with the manufacturer of your computer to install any available BIOS (Basic Input/Output System) firmware. This may help resolving the issue with the performance of your keyboard.  In case that you already have the latest BIOS firmware on your device and the issue still persists, we suggest that you perform a clean install for Windows 10. This will ensure that your computer will be clean from possible software conflicts. Please click here for the steps. Is there any way to modify the below so instead of returning the first non-empty, instead return first Cell that does not say “NO”.  So any cell that has a value other than NO would be the AM1 value.  Below is an example of how It'd hope it would work.  So I just want the first value that appears in the column (and in this case it would beFEL1) to be the value what would appear in Cell AM1.   Lastly, if all that is returned is “NO” and there is no other value, can you have it write an error message “Like No Matches Found in Search.”    Thanks so much for any help and thoughts on how to achieve this –   Jenny B.      SuStacyFirstNonEmptyCell()            Dim startCell As Range, firstNonEmptyCell As Range       Set startCell = Range("AJ:AJ")       If VBA.IsEmpty(startCell.Value) Then         MsgBox "No data in this column"     Else         Set firstNonEmptyCell = startCell.End(xlDown)                   Range("AM1") = firstNonEmptyCell.Value >>> Instead would like it to be first value in Range AJ that is not NO             End If         Call CopyDown                End Sub        A                  B                    C               D          E                F                 AH              AI            AJ                 AK             AL       AM 353514199 L17     F     F     7777788899 NO FEL1 353514102 L17     N     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112222 FEL1 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514199 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     7771112221 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     N     N     3333333222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333332222 NO 353514102 L17     F     F     3333222222 NO 353514C99 L17     E     N     8675309651 FEL1 353514Z99 L17     E     F     7727272727 NO
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I need to modify an existing Excel VBA macro so that instead of returning the first non-empty cell, it searches down column AJ and returns the first cell value that does not equal "NO", placing that result into cell AM1. Additionally, I need the macro to generate a specific error message reading "No Matches Found in Search." if every cell in the column contains "NO" and no other value is found. accepted_answer: This should do the trick best wishes  Sub tryme()   flag = False   LastRow = Cells(Rows.Count, "AJ").End(xlUp).Row   For k = 1 To LastRow     If Range("AJ" & k) <> "NO" Then       Range("m1") = Range("AJ" & k)       flag = True     End If   Next k   If flag = False Then      Range("M1") = "Unsuccessful"   End If End Sub

title: dell 5110 random freezes
original_question: Dell 5110 AMD version with W7 64 bit. This laptop works real crisp when it is not frozen. It is getting random total freezes that don’t seem to be caused by any specific task. It freezes whatever is on the screen, the mouse and keyboard. All that is left is the power button and the led’s. It may happen as soon as windows starts, after a couple hours of use or anywhere in between. Usually it seems to be pretty sudden and rarely gives any warning something is coming up. I have tried many of the things I have found to try and fix this.  Memtest and the Dell hardware tests all pass. The only added program on to what the OS has is chrome. I updated IE. Nothing has been done to likely get any malware but I have run Malwarebytes and superantispyware scans and they are clean. MSE scan comes out clean also. I have all current updates. Drivers are at a minimum and are all fine as far as what device manager says. I have run sfc scan and it always comes up clean. Seatools, Easeus surface test and chkdsk have been run, and all is fine. I have run defrag. When I watch task manager it is not running up the cpu or memory, those stay normal even when it freezes. Temps are not running up either.  This is the second clean install and both ended up with this same problem. Have done additional hd and memory tests and all pass. A video stress test was run and that was all fine. No way known to force bsod to get a memory dump as everything but the start button freezes when this happens. I tried a clean boot and it still freezes. I have had it in safe mode many times, including being on hours like that and no freezes ever. With that and the way it does very little if anything as a warning, no strange video or anything it seems to me this is probably not hardware, it seems to be some OS process? Gotten to be real frustration with time spent with this laptop when I know it is probably something easy for someone that knows how to troubleshoot. Is there some way to actually pull the info out of this pc and determine the real problem going on? Thanks for any help.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM My Dell laptop is experiencing random, total system freezes where the screen, mouse, and keyboard become completely unresponsive, leaving only the power button and LEDs active. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Device: Dell 5110 (AMD version) * OS: Windows 7 64-bit * RAM: Originally factory 2x1GB RAM; recently swapped to a known-good 2x2GB RAM kit. * Software: Chrome is the only added program. Internet Explorer is updated. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * The freezes happen randomly: sometimes as soon as Windows starts, sometimes after hours of use, or anywhere in between. * The freezes are sudden and give no warning or strange video glitches. * Everything on the screen freezes. During one specific freeze, the mouse worked for a short time before dying, but usually, the mouse and keyboard die instantly. * Task Manager shows normal CPU and memory usage right up to and during the freeze. * System temperatures remain normal. * I cannot force a BSOD to get a memory dump because the entire system, including the Start button, is completely frozen. * The freezes interrupt Windows updates. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Performed two clean installs of the OS; both resulted in the same freezing problem. * Attempted a third reinstall using a specific Techguy forum procedure for Windows 7 SP1, but it froze around step 7 while trying to download an update. * Swapped the factory 2x1GB RAM for 2x2GB RAM I had used previously. It seemed to work better at first (survived restarts and hours of use), but eventually froze again. * Ran Memtest, Dell hardware tests, and additional hard drive/memory tests; all passed. * Ran Seatools, Easeus surface test, and chkdsk; all came back fine. * Ran a video stress test; it passed. * Ran Malwarebytes, SUPERAntiSpyware, and Microsoft Security Essentials (MSE) scans; all came up clean. * Ran sfc scans; it initially came up clean, but on a later attempt after swapping the RAM, it froze at 31%. * Ran a disk defrag. * Checked Device Manager; drivers are at a minimum and report as fine. * Tried a clean boot, but the system still freezes. * Booted into Safe Mode many times and left it on for hours. It has never frozen in Safe Mode during startup, operation, or shutdown. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * Because the laptop never freezes in Safe Mode and gives no hardware-like warning signs, I strongly suspect this is an OS process issue rather than a hardware failure. * I want to know if there is a way to decipher the event logs or pull specific error logs to identify exactly what is causing the freeze.
accepted_answer: Hello,  There is a possibility that the issue is caused by a hardware failure. Please confirm how many memory slots installed on your laptop. Upon researching, we've found out that there are 2 DDR3 memory sticks by default. We suggest to try using one memory stick at a time and check if the issue persists. We understand that you've already performed such numbers of test to make sure that the hardware components are working correctly. However, there are times that the results of the test is not accurate. In case you're not comfortable in performing the steps above, it would be best to take your computer to its manufacturer or to a local technician.  You may also check this article to identify other potential causes of the said issue.  We look forward to your response.  Regards.

title: Parsing error
original_question: After starting or restarting my computer I get the following error message once the Desktop comes up: IAStorIcon.exe - Configuration parser error Error parsing C:WindowsMicrosoft.NETFrameworkv4.0.30319configmachine.config Parser returned error 0x80070005. As far a I can see, everything I normally use is working, but it could possibly effect something I use rarely.  My operating system is Windows 7 Pro, SP1. This started after a major (7 update) Microsoft Update on 09/13/2017. Moved from:  Windows / Windows 7 / Windows update, recovery, & backup
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM Every time I start or restart my computer, I receive an IAStorIcon.exe configuration parser error as soon as the desktop loads. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Operating System: Windows 7 Pro, SP1 (x64-based system) * Software: Microsoft Office 2007 Suites SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * Exact error message: `IAStorIcon.exe - Configuration parser error Error parsing C:WindowsMicrosoft.NETFrameworkv4.0.30319configmachine.config Parser returned error 0x80070005.` * The error pops up after everything else on the desktop has finished loading. * The message stays on the screen indefinitely until I manually close it. * Once closed, it does not reappear during that session, but it comes back every single time I start or restart the computer. * Everything I normally use seems to be working fine, but I am concerned this might affect something I use rarely. * The issue began on 09/13/2017. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * The error started occurring immediately after a major Microsoft Update on 09/13/2017, where a batch of 7 updates were installed at once. * The specific updates that loaded were: * 2017-09 Security and Quality Rollup for .NET Framework 3.5.1, 4.5.2, 4.6, 4.6.1, 4.7 on Windows 7 and Server 2008 R2 for x64 (KB4041083) * Security update for Microsoft Office 2007 Suites (KB4011063) * 2017-09 Security Monthly Quality Rollup for Windows 7 for x64 based systems (KB4038777) * Security Update for Microsoft Office (KB3213641) * Security Update for Microsoft Office (KB3213649) * Security Update for Microsoft Office (KB3213646) * Windows Malicious Software Removal Tool x64 (KB890830)
accepted_answer: Long story, short version: If you close it and do NOT restart the computer, does it the error appear again?  Longer version: iastoricon.exe is part of the Intel Rapid Storage Technology suite, RAID technology for hard disk drive data storing. The Intel Rapid Storage Technology software package provides high-performance Serial ATA (SATA) and SATA. [IMHO it's been a pain in the butt since it was first introduced years ago.]  The error you cited points to .NET Framework version 4.x and you just installed at least one (1) security update for same as part of the Sept-17 .NET Framework Security & Quality rollup.  I'm running Win7 but I did NOT install that .NET rollup. However, I've been seeing an IAStorIcon has stopped working prompt every time I start my computer.    I just dismiss (close) it and then all is well during the current boot. [My computer is up & running pretty much 24/7. I might restart it two or three times a month at most.]  It started when I installed a .NET rollup late last year (which also permanently "killed" my Mail Client as well as Event Viewer and Scheduled Tasks). As such, I've chosen NOT to install ANY futher .NET updates since then. YMMV ...
