title: Alternates to EMF vector formats for MS Word
original_question: Hi, I am creating a document with many vector images involving numerous effects. I draw vector images in inkscape, and then convert them in EMF format for use in MS Word. The problem is that many effects like gradient effect and clipping are lost in the process of conversion and the EMF illustration is no longer comparable to original SVG image. The use of vector image is very important and it is very unlikely that I would use a raster image (unless the illustration used is a picture). I've searched the internet for questions asking solutions to same problem, many people recommended the use of power point to create image and then save them as EMF. I have no problem doing that until I can perform all the basic operation that can be done in inkscape (effects like clipping and masking etc.) and there is a clear documentation available on-line for use of MS Drawing tools in Power point. If there is an independent software that creates images in EMF format by default, that would be helpful too.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I am trying to insert vector images with complex effects into MS Word, but when I convert my original SVG files to EMF format, effects like gradients and clipping are lost. I need an alternative vector format that MS Word supports, or a dedicated program that natively creates EMF files without losing these effects. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - MS Word - PowerPoint - Inkscape (used for drawing original vector images) - Adobe Illustrator (mentioned as a standard vector design software) - Apache Office (mentioned for its native SVG support) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - During the conversion from SVG to EMF, gradient effects and clipping are completely lost. - The resulting EMF illustration visually degrades and is no longer comparable to the original SVG image. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED - I currently draw vector images in Inkscape and convert them to EMF format to use in MS Word. - I searched the internet for solutions and found recommendations to use PowerPoint to create images and save them as EMF. - I searched for dedicated EMF graphic designing software but could not find any. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT - Using vector images is strictly required for my document; I will not use raster images unless the illustration is an actual photograph. - I am willing to use PowerPoint as a workaround to create and save images as EMF, but only if I can perform basic Inkscape operations like masking and clipping. - By "clipping," I mean defining a shape to extract a specific part of an original image and cropping everything else (e.g., drawing a circle over a rectangular vector to extract and crop just the circular part). - I need clear online documentation for MS Drawing tools if I am to use PowerPoint for this. - I expected MS Word to have a vector format more compatible with vector design software, given that open-source programs like Apache Office natively support original SVGs.
accepted_answer: Any software that produces EMF will likely have the same kinds of problems.  EMF/WMF graphics are fairly primitive and don't include the ability to handle things like gradient fills, soft edges/shadows and the like.  Instead, programs that export EMF have to perform workarounds; to "preserve" a gradient, they'll export gazillions of rectangles or polygons that, together, look like a single gradient filled shape.  Editing these?  A nightmare!  I would suggest this workflow:  Draw your graphics in any program that works well for you, but stick with simple shapes and lines.  Don't do any gradient fills or other fancy effects.  Export as EMF, import the EMF into PowerPoint, ungroup it and THEN apply any effects you like to the shapes.  Once you do this, the graphic will be in native MS Office format; you can copy/paste it from PPT to Word and it should retain all of its characteristics.  You might, in fact, be able to import the EMF directly into Word, ungroup and apply effects there.  Since PPT is the tool I use most often, I'd rather work there and then copy to Word, but if going directly to Word works well for you, leave PPT out of the equation entirely.

title: remsh.exe started to appear in my Task Manager and to have high CPU usage

I am trying to determine if this is a legitimate Microsoft process or a malware infection, and I need to stop it from using high CPU all day. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * OS: Windows 10, build 1511 (x64 architecture) * Antivirus/Security: Windows Defender (primary), Malwarebytes, and Zemana * Utilities: CCleaner (an older version, prior to the compromised release) * Hardware constraints: My PC is not compatible with the latest Windows 10 updates/upgrades. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * remsh.exe runs with high CPU usage. * The issue started two days ago. * The process starts alongside Telemetry, which never happened before. Telemetry also had two processes open this time. * I found a folder located at C:\Program Files\rempl that was created last month. * The process does not have an associated service in the Services tab. * The three files in the rempl folder have the exact creation dates and file sizes mentioned in a Microsoft support article (KB4023057) under the x64 tab. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * I ended the remsh.exe process in Task Manager. * I restarted Windows. During the reboot, the OS made some updates. * I disabled Windows updates completely due to my hardware incompatibility. * I searched Google and found conflicting information stating remsh.exe could be a Microsoft process or an infection. * I cross-referenced my file attributes, dates, and sizes with Microsoft support article KB4023057. accepted_answer: This is not a native Microsoft process. Click the Startup tab in the Task Manager, then deactivate this program. When you right-click the folder C:\Program Files\rempl then you can see when you created it. This should give you a clue about its purpose.

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO OS: Windows 8.1 (x64) Browser: Internet Explorer 11 (IE11) Product: Bing Maps website Other Browsers Tested: Firefox SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When printing, the bottom of a page is partially cut off (though the footer still prints), and at the top of the next page, an entire step is cut off (though the header still prints). Specifically, the miles/time badges get cut off between the bottom of one page and the top of the next. In one example, 2 out of 6 pages had this issue, even though there was more than enough room on the final page to push everything down. The issue occurs whether I print just the directions, or the directions and maps together. They paginate differently, but both have the exact same cutoff problem on different pages. There is no option available to adjust or eliminate the space taken by the header or footer to fix the pagination. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I verified that "Shrink to fit" was checked in my print settings. I tried printing at different percentages, but there was no change from 100% and it did not prevent the cutoffs. I installed the latest Windows 8 x64 driver for my printer, which helped slightly but did not completely resolve the issue. I used the F12 developer tools in IE11 and changed the emulation from the default IE7 to IE8, which successfully eliminated the pagination issue. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I inspected the page source and found `<meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=7" />` in the `<head>`, which restricts IE to IE7 document mode. Firefox prints the pages correctly, which makes sense since it ignores that IE-specific meta tag.
accepted_answer: Hi,  Thank you for keeping us updated.  I believe the issue that you are facing is an implementing one (As you have aptly guessed). The issue is because the Bing maps directions webpage defaults to IE 7 mode. You can let our Product Design Team aware of this issue by providing the feedback in the below link:  http://connect.microsoft.com/IE

title: Receiving file with same name
original_question: Hello, I noticed there's a bug when someone sends you a file multiple times with the same name, if you click the link for file received in Skype window you get the very first file that was sent but Explorer renames the file to add (1), (2) and so on and it's confusing. How can I report it this problem? I got lots of "Skype FAQ" pages but no direct link. Best regards, David.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM When I receive a file multiple times with the exact same name, clicking the "file received" link directly in the Skype window opens the very first version of the file that was sent, rather than the most recently received version. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Software: Skype for Windows Desktop OS Component: Windows Explorer SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When a file is sent multiple times with the same name, Windows Explorer correctly saves the newer files by appending (1), (2), etc., to the filenames. However, the Skype chat window link does not point to the newly renamed file; it points to the original file. Because of this, I recently opened an updated report document via Skype and missed important added information because it opened the older version. I first noticed this problem a while ago and have not checked today to see if it has been fixed. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I searched through many "Skype FAQ" pages to find a direct link to report this bug, but I could not find one. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT I know the renaming behavior in Windows Explorer is intentional and useful. My current workaround is to right-click the file in Skype, select "Show in folder", and open the latest file directly via Explorer. I have to warn everyone to do this, which is annoying and wastes time. I think Skype should either link to the renamed version or create new folders named by date and time (e.g., "2017_09_28_14_35_58") for received files so obsolete files don't make finding the newest file so difficult.
accepted_answer: I'm not sure why this happens for you, but I have several files with the same name (DxDiag.txt) saved in my "My Skype Received Files" folder. Each has the extra index attached, but when I click on the file in Skype, the correct version opens. This is how it should work.  Links to received/sent files are saved in the local main.db database file, and should point to the correct version.

title: Wrong place in Bing weather
original_question: I have a strange issue. I have a Samsung smart PC and my father a Dell Latitude 10 both with W8 pro Updt. 1. We have an home in Menton PACA France so we have put the place in the Bing Weather favorites. My father started to say that his Menton is wrong, on the Weather map it's in the wrong place (more the 100km away) and he thinks even the temperatures are wrong. I checked on my tablet and on the weather map is in the correct place and we have different temperatures and even the skiing place are different (his skiing place are far haway), both updated on the same time. There is only one Menton in France so the place is correct. I tried removing the place, uninstalling the app but on his tablet is still wrong. I just found that the same issue happen on my sister desktop but on all my 4 Windows 8 device it's correct, even on my WP8 it's correct. Maybe I used the Current Place function in the app once I was there but I'm not sure, anyway there is something very wrong here. This is my sister weather map: http://1drv.ms/1kbRYwc This is my weather map: http://1drv.ms/1kbS9HY How is possible something like that? How can I solve this issue? Thanks
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM The Bing Weather app is displaying the location for Menton, France in the wrong place on the map (over 100km away) on some of my family's devices. Because the map location is wrong, the reported temperatures and nearby skiing locations are also incorrect. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * App: Bing Weather * My device: Samsung smart PC running Windows 8 Pro Update 1 * My father's device: Dell Latitude 10 running Windows 8 Pro Update 1 * My sister's device: Windows 8 Desktop PC * My other devices: 4 total Windows 8 devices and a Windows Phone 8 (WP8) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * We added our holiday home in Menton, PACA, France to the Bing Weather favorites. * On my father's tablet and my sister's desktop, the weather map shows Menton in the wrong location, more than 100km away. * The temperatures and nearby skiing places shown on their devices are completely different from mine (their listed skiing places are far away). * Both the correct and incorrect devices are updating at the exact same time. * There is only one Menton in France, so there is no ambiguity with the town name. * I have screenshots comparing my sister's incorrect weather map (http://1drv.ms/1kbRYwc) with my correct weather map (http://1drv.ms/1kbS9HY). WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Removed the location from the app and re-added it. * Uninstalled and reinstalled the Bing Weather app on the affected tablet. * Ran the Windows troubleshooter and completed all standard troubleshooting steps. * Tested searching for the location using the post code on my sister's PC, which successfully showed the correct position. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * All of our PCs are connected to the same network. * The location displays perfectly fine on my Samsung tablet, all 4 of my Windows 8 devices, and my WP8 phone. * We do not live in Menton, so I cannot easily use the "Current Location" GPS feature to fix the desktop PC. * I suspect my tablet shows the correct location because I might have used the "Current Location" function in the app while I was physically visiting Menton in the past, but I am not entirely sure.
accepted_answer: When you type in Current Location, you may type the City, town you live in rather than a post code.  These are the stats I have for Menton Paca France (Fig1), I changed from Farenheit to Celsius (Fig2) and (Fig3) shows Menton Paca France location.         If the IP address is not detected, the weather map will locate the server closes to your location and this may be as an Example:  You're in Marseile but the server is detected in Barcelona.  Right click on  Start and select Command Prompt (Admin) Type ipconfig /flushdns Press enter  Retry setting the location in Weather App

title: Powerpoint for iOS: iPhone Laser pointer feature question
original_question: Hi I just read this article Announcing September feature update for Office for iPhone and iPad (version 1.25) and I can't quite get how the Powerpoint for iOS laser pointer feature works. The article reads: "A new way to use the laser pointer: In PowerPoint Presenter view, point your phone at the screen, then press and hold in the Notes area to turn your device into a laser pointer. (Feature requires an Office 365 subscription and is available only on iPhones" Do I have a slide deck loaded in presenter view on my laptop and have the iPhone connected via bluetooth to act as a laser pointer? And if so where in the notes area of which device (laptop?). The PowerPoint app is connected to my work Office365 subscription. Could anyone point me to a more elaborate explanation? Thank you in advance Panos
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I read about the new laser pointer feature in the September update for Office for iPhone and iPad, but I do not understand how it works or how to actually set it up. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO - Devices: iPhone, Laptop, Projectors/HDTVs - Software: PowerPoint for iOS / Office for iPhone and iPad (version 1.25) - Account: Work Office 365 subscription SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS - The update article states: "In PowerPoint Presenter view, point your phone at the screen, then press and hold in the Notes area to turn your device into a laser pointer." - It is completely unclear to me how this functions practically. I do not know if I am supposed to have the slide deck loaded in presenter view on my laptop, if the iPhone needs to be connected via Bluetooth to act as the pointer, or which device's "Notes area" I am supposed to press and hold. accepted_answer: The subject title is a little misleading. It doesn't actually turn your phone into a laser pointer. Instead, when you are using an iPhone hooked to a projector, you can touch the screen of the iPhone and a red dot will appear on the projected image (you can't see it on the iPhone because your finger tip covers it). You're showing the deck from your iPhone, not a separate computer.

SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO Office 365 (specifically Word and Excel). I use this software across several devices. I log into my computer using a Microsoft account and password. I have OneDrive, but clicking the OneDrive "app" in the Start menu just opens File Explorer with OneDrive selected. SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS When I attempt to save, a box pops up with the exact message: "tell us what account you want to use to open _vti_rpc" and asks me to sign in. When I sign in with my email address and Microsoft password, it just takes me back to the original box. This is a fairly recent occurrence. I also no longer see the "changes being saved" message along the bottom of the screen that I used to see. When I check Properties/Sharing for OneDrive, there is no mention of Office and it says "OneDrive not shared". WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED I tried signing in with my email and computer password, but the prompt just loops. I checked my software updates; despite having the option to automatically download and update selected, I was not completely up to date. I manually requested and carried out an update. I also searched for a standalone OneDrive app in the Start apps list but could only find the File Explorer shortcut. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT The error does not actually stop the changes from being saved; I can subsequently access the updated documents from my other devices. Recently, the problem seems to have stopped occurring on its own, and I am currently unable to reproduce the issue.
accepted_answer: Thank you for responding. The OneDrive application is located at the lower right portion of your screen near the clock. If it's not visible, click the arrow pointing upward. It should open a little box where you will find the OneDrive app logo which is represented by an icon of a cloud.    Please refer to the support link provided below regarding certain issues with their corresponding fixes or workarounds. This link was last updated September 25, 2017 to ensure effectiveness of solutions posted.  Fixes or workarounds for recent issues in Excel for Windows.  Let us know the result for us to continue assisting you.  Regards.

title: Bing Maps Desktop Default Location Issue
original_question: Hello, I would like to have the default location of Bing Maps to be my home instead of zoomed out of the world.   I am running a Windows 7 machine and I'm using desktop Bing Maps.  I changed the default location in my Bing settings and I've turn on location sharing through my control panel.  I've changed browsers between Chrome and IE and it didn't change anything.   Any other suggestions on how I can fix this?  Thank you in advance.
simulator_context: CORE PROBLEM I want the default location of Bing Maps to be my home, but it always opens zoomed out to a view of the entire world. SYSTEM & SOFTWARE INFO * Operating System: Windows 7 * Device: Desktop computer * Browsers: Internet Explorer (set as my default browser) and Google Chrome * Application: Bing Maps accessed via the desktop URL (I am not using the Windows app) SYMPTOMS & ERROR DETAILS * When I navigate to the Bing Maps URL, the map shows a default view of the entire world. * I have to scroll 3 to 4 times just to zoom in on my general area. * There are no error codes or error messages. WHAT I HAVE ALREADY TRIED * Changed the default location in my Bing settings. * Turned on location sharing through my Windows Control Panel location settings. * Tested the issue in both Internet Explorer and Chrome to see if it was browser-specific. * Changed settings through my Microsoft account. * Verified that my computer's Time Zone/Region settings are set to Pacific, which perfectly matches my Bing settings. ADDITIONAL CONTEXT * I am experiencing a separate but possibly related issue on my phone app, which shows my default location as 1,000 miles away and gives me news and weather for that distant area. * As a workaround, I have been using Google Maps because it automatically zooms in on my location without requiring manual scrolling.
accepted_answer: Hi Ryan,  First of all, I would like to apologize for the delay in responding.  I would like to know some more information about this issue. 1. Are you travelling? 2. Where is your exact location?  If you have already tried changing the region settings in Bing settings, then there might any mismatch with the Time Zone/Region in the computer settings and Bing settings. I suggest you to make sure that the Time Zone/Region settings in your computer is same in Bing settings.  Refer to the following Microsoft Help article to confirm the region setting. http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/change-country-region-setting#1TC=windows-7  You may also refer to the following Microsoft Help article to make changes in the Time Zone. http://windows.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/set-clock#1TC=windows-7  Please reply with the status of the issue so that we can help you fix this issue.
